Out-of-scope by the bead's own definition: dynamic watchlist derivation, theme membership inference, bulk overlay (those belong to P4.7).

title: P1.5 Portfolio holdings model & overlay inputs
description:
# P1.5 — Portfolio holdings model & overlay inputs

## What

Lightweight portfolio research context (not brokerage execution): tracks held exposure for overlays and monitoring. A `Portfolio` owns one explicit `base_currency` interpreting cost assumptions and overlay totals. `PortfolioHolding` binds to canonical market identity (typically `instrument` or `listing`), plus quantity, optional cost basis, and open/close timestamps.

## Why

Portfolio overlays need a defined input contract. Without explicit `base_currency` and canonical-identity binding, overlay math sprays implicit FX assumptions and ticker-string persistence across surfaces.

## Contract / acceptance

- Portfolio support is lightweight research context: tracks held exposure for overlays and monitoring — NOT brokerage execution, order management, tax lots, cash ledgers, settlement.
- `Portfolio.base_currency` is required and defines reporting currency for holding cost assumptions and overlay totals.
- `base_currency` is a reporting/comparison assumption, not proof that the underlying listing trades in that currency, and not a full FX accounting model.
- `PortfolioHolding` binds to canonical market identity (`instrument` or `listing`) — must NOT persist raw ticker strings or higher-order subjects (theme, macro_topic, portfolio, screen) as holding identity.
- `cost_basis` is optional; when present, interpreted in the portfolio's `base_currency`. No separate transaction currencies, FX lots, or fee-adjusted basis accounting.
- Overlay inputs from holdings are read models keyed by subject + contributing portfolio — not new canonical subject identities or stored UI payloads.
- Minimum overlay input contract: held-state, contributing `portfolio_id`, quantity, optional cost basis, base-currency label.
- Multiple portfolios holding the same subject with different base currencies: contributions stay distinct (no silent netting through implicit FX).
- Manual watchlists and holdings are separate: saving a subject does not create a holding; holding a subject does not add it to a watchlist.

## Depends on

- P0.4b (manual watchlist baseline)
- P0.4 (subject entry)

## Downstream consumers

- P1.5b (first surface behaviors)
- P4.7 (dynamic watchlists + portfolio overlays — consume the overlay input contract)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Do NOT model margin, realized tax, fees, or order history. This is research, not brokerage.
- Use `subject_kind + subject_id` polymorphism; enforce allowed kinds (instrument, listing) in application code.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/portfolio/HoldingsService.ts`
- `/v1/portfolios/*` handlers
close_reason: P1.5 delivered: Portfolio model (.9.1) with required base_currency, PortfolioHolding (.9.2) bound to instrument/listing canonical identity (theme/screen/etc rejected), and overlay-input read model (.9.3) keyed by subject + contributing portfolio with no implicit FX netting. Service at services/portfolio with HTTP CRUD plus POST /v1/portfolios/overlays. Downstream consumer fra-cw0.10 (P1.5b first surface behaviors) is now unblocked.

title: P1.3 Symbol detail (overview, financials, earnings core tabs)
description:
# P1.3 — Symbol detail (overview, financials, earnings core tabs)

## What

Symbol detail's three core deterministic sections: `overview` (extends the thin quote landing into a durable core tab with profile + key stats + limited performance context), `financials` (normalized statement tables, statement-linked trend views, segment-aware breakdowns), `earnings` (deterministic earnings chronology, expectation-vs-result views, consensus summaries).

## Why

These three tabs are the deterministic core of the symbol workspace — the foundation that Analyze (P4.2), Home deep-links (P4.4), and chat pivots all rely on. Fixing their ownership boundaries here prevents later surfaces from collapsing them into one scrolling page or duplicating ownership.

## Contract / acceptance

- `overview` composes listing-aware quote context, company profile, key stats, limited performance/context summary. Does NOT become a second home for full statement tables, holders, or interpretive evidence flows.
- `financials` owns normalized statement tables + statement-linked trends + segment-aware breakdowns. Composes normalized statements + aggregation layer for key stats + segment facts. Does NOT rebuild fundamentals logic from provider-specific payloads.
- `earnings` owns earnings chronology + expectation-vs-result + consensus summaries. Composes earnings-release events + EPS surprise + consensus + price-target context. Does NOT turn transcript reading, news clustering, or freeform commentary into tab-owned responsibilities.
- All three tabs live inside the subject-detail shell and share subject header context + nested-route navigation.
- Core tab composition depends on hydrated subject identity, market quote/series services, fundamentals profile/statement services, aggregation outputs, and structured earnings events — through backend contracts, not direct provider payloads or chat-style tool loops.
- Moving between overview/financials/earnings preserves subject context and shell chrome — local section transition, not a new top-level workspace.
- Tabs may link between each other through stable section destinations. Must not collapse into one scrolling page.

## Depends on

- P0.4 (landing state to extend)
- P1.1 + P1.1b (market data + series)
- P1.2 + P1.2b (fundamentals + aggregations)

## Downstream consumers

- P1.3b (holders, signals, Analyze entry layered on top)
- P4.2 (Analyze launches from symbol detail preserving subject context)
- P4.4 (Home deep-links into the right deterministic tab)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Do NOT use chat-style tool loops inside symbol tabs. These are deterministic surfaces.
- Earnings tab must distinguish reported earnings (Fact) from consensus/estimate (aggregate).

## Files to touch (likely)

- `app/symbol/overview/*`
- `app/symbol/financials/*`
- `app/symbol/earnings/*`
close_reason: Closed

title: P1.3b Symbol detail (holders, signals, Analyze entry integration)
description:
# P1.3b — Symbol detail (holders, signals, Analyze entry integration)

## What

The adjacent symbol-detail sections and launch points layered onto P1.3's core: `holders` (institutional + insider holder views), `signals` (community views, news pulse, future alt-data — extensible route bucket), plus explicit handoff from symbol detail into top-level `Analyze` with carried `SubjectRef` context.

## Why

Holders, signals, and Analyze entry are first-class symbol-detail affordances in the videos. Keeping them distinct from the core tabs (overview/financials/earnings) preserves those tabs' deterministic ownership and prevents scope creep.

## Contract / acceptance

- `holders` composes structured holder outputs from the fundamentals service. Remains distinct from portfolio overlays, watchlist state, and user-owned monitoring.
- `signals` is the symbol-detail section for Reddit-like community views, news pulse, future alt-data. This bead does NOT replace that route bucket with a source-specific `/reddit` shell.
- Reddit-like/news-specialized content inside `signals` composes evidence-backed blocks, claim clusters, evidence bundles, and trend-style renderers — not raw social-text panes or provider-specific mini-pages.
- `Analyze` remains a top-level workspace, NOT a durable nested symbol-detail section.
- Symbol detail launches `Analyze` with carried `SubjectRef` context or an explicit analyze intent. Resulting artifacts live on the shared snapshot + block plane, not inside a persistent symbol-detail tab.
- Entering `holders` or `signals` preserves subject context inside the same nested-route family and subject-detail shell.
- Launching `Analyze` from symbol detail is a workspace transition that still preserves carried subject context (no reinterpretation from raw ticker text).
- Handoff into `Analyze` stays explicit so later shared-artifact/replay flows point to durable snapshot-backed artifacts (not scraped UI state).

## Depends on

- P1.3 (core tabs)

## Downstream consumers

- P4.2 (Analyze template system — receives carried subject context from this handoff)
- P4.3 (shared artifact flow — consumes sealed artifacts produced by Analyze entry)
- P4.6 (specialized social/news blocks — live under `signals` and reuse evidence-backed blocks)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Naming discipline: `signals` NOT `reddit` even if the current vendor content is mostly Reddit. The route bucket must survive vendor swaps.
- Analyze launches from symbol detail carry the `SubjectRef`, not a ticker string.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `app/symbol/holders/*`
- `app/symbol/signals/*`
- `app/analyze/entry.ts` (handoff)

title: P1.4 Screener query model & result contract
description:
# P1.4 — Screener query model & result contract

## What

The service-owned screener query surface: structured filter-and-rank envelopes (not a freeform DSL), bound to screener-owned fields backed by market-data and fundamentals services. Defines the result-row shape and the persisted `screen` subject (replayable query definition + ordering semantics).

## Why

Screener consumers (UI, dynamic watchlists, agents, theme derivation) must see one query envelope shape. Allowing arbitrary provider-payload joins or client-side DSLs multiplies bug surface and breaks the single-source principle.

## Contract / acceptance

- Query envelopes are explicit structured filter-and-rank, not freeform DSL or user-authored formulas.
- Minimum query dimensions: universe constraints, market/quote constraints, fundamentals/aggregate constraints, sort spec, page/limit.
- Query clauses bind to screener-owned fields backed by market-data and fundamentals services — no raw provider payload columns, no frontend-computed joins.
- Screener response is an ordered derived result set — not a new canonical identity type.
- Each result row carries canonical market subject identity, display identity, ranking/sort context, and compact quote/fundamentals summaries sufficient for screener-table rendering.
- Screener rows are thinner than symbol-detail hydration: selecting a row hands off canonical subject identity for symbol-entry flow. Does NOT embed a full symbol workspace payload.
- Reusable `screen` subject represents the persisted query definition + ordering semantics — NOT a frozen list of prehydrated row payloads.
- Service owns: query validation, execution, ranking, pagination, row-envelope assembly.
- `/v1/screener/*` is the client boundary. Clients must NOT fan out to `/v1/market/*` + `/v1/fundamentals/*` and invent join semantics.

## Depends on

- P1.1, P1.1b (market data)
- P1.2, P1.2b (fundamentals)

## Downstream consumers

- P1.4b (screener surface flow + saved-screen handoff)
- P4.1 (themes — screen-to-theme handoff)
- P4.7 (dynamic watchlists — replay screen definition)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Saving a screen persists the query definition, not a snapshot of rows. Rehydrating reruns the query — this is the whole point of replayability.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/screener/QueryService.ts`
- `services/screener/Ranker.ts`
- `/v1/screener/*` handlers

Result-row envelope carrying canonical SubjectRef + display identity + sort context + compact summaries (cw0.7.2). Persistable 'screen' subject as replayable query definition + ordering semantics, NOT a frozen row cache (cw0.7.3). title: P1.2 Fundamentals service (statement & metric normalization)
description:
# P1.2 — Fundamentals service (statement & metric normalization)

## What

The fundamentals-service layer that turns filing-backed or vendor-backed statement inputs into canonical value objects keyed by metric definitions. Owns the three core statement families (`income`, `balance`, `cashflow`), fiscal-calendar normalization, period/unit/scale normalization, and the mapping from normalized statement lines to canonical `Metric` definitions.

## Why

Displayed statement values must resolve to `Fact` rows when directly observed or promoted, and to `Computation` rows when deterministically derived. Without the fundamentals service as a hard boundary, every surface invents its own statement-cleanup logic, and I1 (no displayed number without a backing row) is silently violated.

## Contract / acceptance

- Statement reads begin from issuer-appropriate subject context, not listing identity or ticker-only lookup.
- Three statement families normalized explicitly: `income`, `balance`, `cashflow`.
- Statement basis is explicit: `as_reported` | `as_restated` — must not be silently merged.
- Period selection, fiscal labels, scale normalization, unit normalization are part of this contract, not caller cleanup.
- `Metric` is a definition object (not a mutable value store).
- Displayed statement values resolve to `Fact` when directly observed/promoted, to `Computation` when derived.
- Statement normalization must not introduce a second truth layer — no UI-only statement cells or provider-specific blobs that bypass `Fact`/`Computation`.
- Coverage/verification state preserved via canonical value objects when source material is incomplete, conflicting, or pending promotion.
- For US issuers, SEC EDGAR is the primary-source anchor (`data.sec.gov` unauthenticated JSON APIs).
- Non-custom XBRL company facts aggregates do NOT cover segment data, issuer-specific extension facts, or non-GAAP logic — those need separate extraction (P6.1 refines).

## Depends on

- P0.3b (issuer-appropriate subject handoff)
- P0.5 (schema — `metrics`, `facts`, `computations`)

## Downstream consumers

- P1.2b (stats/segment/consensus aggregation)
- P1.3 (financials + earnings tabs)
- P2.2 (pre-resolve router — fundamentals cost class)
- P3.5 (promotion rules — normalized statement values land as Fact/Computation)
- P6.2 (non-US expansion extends accounting mappings)

## Considerations / gotchas

- `data.sec.gov`'s companyfacts is NOT segment-aware. Plan extraction infrastructure for P6.1.
- Restatements are supersessions, not overwrites — use `facts.supersedes` / `superseded_by`.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/fundamentals/StatementService.ts`
- `services/fundamentals/MetricMapper.ts`
- `services/fundamentals/sec/*`
- `/v1/fundamentals/statements` handler
- `/v1/fundamentals/profile` handler
close_reason: Roll-up: all four children landed and merged via PR #19 (fra-cw0.3.1 statement normalization, fra-cw0.3.2 fiscal calendar, fra-cw0.3.3 metric mapper, fra-cw0.3.4 SEC EDGAR primary-source anchor). Fundamentals service produces canonical NormalizedStatement / MappedStatement value objects with explicit basis/period/scale/unit, AAPL FY2024 income statement verifies end-to-end through SEC companyfacts → metric registry.

title: P1.2b Stats, segment, and consensus aggregations
description:
# P1.2b — Stats, segment, and consensus aggregations

## What

The fundamentals aggregation layer above normalized statement facts that produces reusable read models for key stats, segment facts, analyst consensus, and comparison-ready derived outputs. Aggregations expose their derivation inputs, freshness, and coverage assumptions explicitly.

## Why

Without a shared aggregation layer, every UI surface builds ad-hoc ratio/target logic from raw statements, duplicating work and drifting from canonical values. Keeping aggregations derivation-transparent preserves I1 — the consumer can always trace a displayed aggregate back to the inputs that produced it.

## Contract / acceptance

- Aggregation outputs are service-level views, not replacements for `Fact` or `Computation` rows.
- Key stats and derived ratios may combine fundamentals, market context, and deterministic computations — must expose basis, period, and `as_of` assumptions.
- Segment facts remain distinct from consolidated statement outputs: carry `axis`, `segment_definitions`, period context, `coverage_warnings`. Do not flatten segment disclosures into issuer-level statement tables.
- Analyst consensus remains distinct from both reported statements and promoted evidence facts — rating distributions, price-target summaries, analyst counts, coverage warnings with explicit `as_of`.
- Comparison-ready derived outputs may package reusable aggregate slices for peer views — must not become opaque caches of UI-specific payloads.
- Incomplete, stale, or inconsistent inputs surface warnings + partial-coverage metadata — never fabricate complete comparisons.
- May read canonical facts, computations, and provider-backed consensus/segment inputs — provenance, supersession, and truth-promotion state remain owned by the canonical value plane.

## Depends on

- P1.2 (normalized statement layer)

## Downstream consumers

- P1.3 (overview, financials, earnings reuse these read models)
- P2.2 (router classifies heavier fundamentals requests)
- P4.6 (specialized blocks cite stable aggregate envelopes)
- P6.1 (segment extraction refinement extends segment aggregates)
- P6.2 (non-US expansion — aggregates must keep basis/freshness explicit)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Analyst consensus is NOT an observed fact about the issuer — it's an aggregate of analyst opinion. Keep the distinction in the block and in labels.
- Peer comparison aggregates can be heavy — consider caching at this level (cache identity: peer-set × metric × basis × period).

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/fundamentals/aggregations/*`
- `/v1/fundamentals/stats` handler
- `/v1/fundamentals/segments` handler
- `/v1/fundamentals/consensus` handler
close_reason: Roll-up: all three children landed (fra-cw0.4.1 key-stats, fra-cw0.4.2 segment-facts, fra-cw0.4.3 analyst-consensus) and are open as PRs #21 / #22 / #23 against main. Each aggregation envelope is derivation-transparent (per-input source_id + as_of), distinct from canonical Fact rows, and emits structured coverage_warnings rather than fabricating coverage. The 'comparison-ready derived outputs' clause is intentionally deferred — peer comparison aggregates will land with P4.x consumers when the consumer surface is concrete enough to drive the cache identity.

title: P1.1 Market data service (quote & bar provider abstraction)
description:
# P1.1 — Market data service (quote & bar provider abstraction)

## What

The sole internal boundary for quote and bar provider interaction. Downstream consumers (symbol detail, chat tools, watchlist rows, screener) call the market data service or analyst tools — never provider SDKs, raw provider endpoints, or provider-specific payload helpers. Provider adapters normalize vendor-specific shapes, identifiers, rate limits, and availability quirks into stable internal market records before they reach downstream consumers.

## Why

Provider lock-in is the single most common source of reliability risk in finance data. A provider-neutral market-data seam lets us swap providers, run fallbacks, and layer caching without changing consumer code. It also prevents ticker-string shortcuts — consumers must pass listing-appropriate hydrated subject context, not raw ticker text.

## Contract / acceptance

- Quote and bar retrieval start from listing-appropriate hydrated subject context (`listing` SubjectRef), **not** raw tickers, venue text, or provider identifiers.
- Provider-neutral contract preserves `as_of`, `delay_class`, `currency`, `source_id` for all quote/bar responses; bars also carry `adjustment_basis`.
- Quote retrieval: latest price, absolute move, percentage move, freshness/session state — lightweight and snapshot-oriented. Does NOT answer historical range questions, adjusted-series questions, or comparison normalization (those belong to P1.1b).
- Bar retrieval: ordered intraday/historical OHLCV for a listing across a requested range/interval; exposes `adjustment_basis` whenever a response has crossed an adjustment boundary.
- Provider failures, stale data, or missing coverage surface as normalized availability outcomes — never raw provider error payloads leaking to callers.
- When the hydrated subject bundle also carries issuer context, quote retrieval still resolves the active market snapshot from listing context.

## Depends on

- P0.3b (hydrated subject handoff — listing-appropriate)
- P0.5 (schema + `sources` table seeded)

## Downstream consumers

- P1.1b (adjusted series + caching — layered above this)
- P1.3 (symbol detail quote + bar surfaces)
- P2.2 (pre-resolve router uses cost class to choose quote vs bar path)
- P6.5 (scale hardening audits at this boundary)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Corporate actions belong to the market data service but their semantics are deferred to P1.1b. This bead only needs adapters to *not lose* corporate-action info in normalization.
- Venue-sensitive session interpretation (regular vs pre/post market) lives here — don't push it downstream.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/market/adapters/*` (per-provider)
- `services/market/QuoteService.ts`
- `services/market/BarService.ts`
- `/v1/market/quote` + `/v1/market/bars` handlers
close_reason: All six child beads (fra-cw0.1.1 through fra-cw0.1.6) closed; market data service shipped.

title: P0.6 Dev infra & observability skeleton
description:
# P0.6 — Dev infra & observability skeleton

## What

Bootstrap the development infrastructure: CI, test runner, lint/typecheck, local dev shell, feature-flag harness, plus a skeleton of the observability tables (`tool_call_logs`, `citation_logs`, `verifier_fail_logs`, `eval_run_results`) and a minimal SSE framework for chat streaming.

## Why

Without observability from day one, regressions in invariant-sensitive code paths are invisible. The spec explicitly names these log tables as day-one necessities — not afterthoughts — because evals drift is hard to detect without baselines.

## Contract / acceptance

- CI runs typecheck, lint, and tests on every PR.
- Local dev shell can run the full backend + frontend + postgres + redis with one command.
- Feature-flag harness exists (used for gating P6 features in dev).
- `tool_call_logs`, `citation_logs`, `verifier_fail_logs`, `eval_run_results` exist per schema pack and are writable.
- Golden-eval scaffolding exists (even if empty) so PX.1 can expand it.
- SSE route `/v1/chat/threads/:threadId/stream` exists as a stub (real wiring in P2.1).

## Depends on

- P0.5 (schema must be applied)

## Downstream consumers

- All phases depend on observability.
- PX.1 extends this skeleton with real evals and drift monitoring.
- P2.1 replaces the stub SSE route with the real streaming implementation.

## Considerations / gotchas

- Keep CI fast. Golden evals belong to a scheduled job, not every PR.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- `package.json` / `turbo.json` / `pnpm-workspace.yaml`
- `observability/logs/*`
- `evals/golden/*` (empty skeleton)
close_reason: Completed dev infra and observability skeleton: CI, observability log tables/write helpers, SSE stream stub, full-stack dev shell, feature-flag harness, and golden-eval starter scaffolding are all tracked by closed child beads fra-6al.8.1 through fra-6al.8.6.

title: P0.3b Search-to-subject resolution flow
description:
# P0.3b — Search-to-subject resolution flow

## What

The deterministic orchestration that carries lookup input from candidate search through canonical selection into downstream-safe subject hydration: **candidate search → canonical selection → hydrated subject handoff**.

## Why

Candidate search may return zero, one, or many candidates. Without a disciplined flow, downstream consumers invent their own winner-picking, producing identity drift. The hydrated bundle is the single shape watchlists, chat, Analyze entry, and service calls all consume.

## Contract / acceptance

- Unique deterministic candidate satisfying the resolver contract may auto-advance without a separate chooser step.
- Multiple plausible candidates pause at explicit ambiguity preservation and surface ranked candidates for later user/caller choice.
- `not_found` ends the flow without subject hydration.
- Only `resolved` produces hydrated subject handoff.
- Hydrated subject bundle contains: canonical `SubjectRef`, resolved identity level, stable display labels, normalized lookup input, resolution path (`auto_advanced` | `explicit_choice`), sufficient issuer/instrument/listing context for immediate downstream use.
- Downstream systems persist only the canonical `SubjectRef`; other bundle fields are entry context that may be refreshed later.

## Depends on

- P0.3 (resolver service)

## Downstream consumers

- P0.4 (symbol search surface)
- P1.1 (market data quote/bar retrieval)
- P1.2 (fundamentals statement normalization)
- P2.2 (pre-resolve router in chat turn loop)
- Watchlist + saved-subject entry flows

## Considerations / gotchas

- Resolution path (`auto_advanced` vs `explicit_choice`) is useful telemetry — log it via PX.1.
- One hydrated bundle may carry joined issuer + active listing context. The canonical `SubjectRef` remains the durable key.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/resolver/flow.ts`
- `app/search/SearchToSubject.tsx`
- `/v1/subjects/resolve` (handler wiring)
close_reason: All P0.3b search-to-subject resolution flow subtasks are complete: candidate search to canonical selection to hydrated handoff, hydrated bundle/API contract, resolution-path telemetry, issuer name/alias search, alias target expansion, and indexed alias lookup. Verified child work with resolver/db test suites during the completed subtasks.

title: P0.4 Symbol search & first quote snapshot surface
description:
# P0.4 — Symbol search & first quote snapshot surface

## What

Shell-owned symbol search entry that, on successful resolution, enters symbol detail with a minimum quote snapshot landing state. The snapshot is the initial landing state of entered symbol detail — not a competing top-level workspace or a detached quote page.

## Why

The videos show ticker entry flows that immediately land on a subject page showing price, move, session state, and a small recent chart. That experience depends on the shell owning search so downstream surfaces (watchlists, portfolio, chat composer) reuse the same search contract instead of redefining symbol entry per surface.

## Contract / acceptance

- Shell owns the primary symbol search entry.
- Unique deterministic hits auto-advance; ambiguous hits require explicit choice; `not_found` ends without subject hydration.
- Successful subject resolution enters symbol detail (main canvas swaps into subject-detail shell while outer workspace shell persists).
- Minimum quote snapshot landing content: canonical subject display identity, listing-sensitive trading symbol context, latest price, absolute move, percentage move, freshness/session state, small recent-range or chart hook.
- Quote retrieval is listing-oriented even when the hydrated subject also carries issuer context.
- A light issuer summary/profile companion is allowed when available but is best-effort and must not block landing state.
- This bead does not define earnings, holders, filings, peer tables, or a finished overview surface.

## Depends on

- P0.3b (search-to-subject flow)
- P1.1 (quote retrieval contract) — note: the snapshot surface is defined here but the underlying quote call lives in P1.1; until P1.1 exists, this surface can stub.

## Downstream consumers

- P0.4b (manual watchlist — reuses search entry + selected-subject handoff)
- P1.3 (symbol overview shell) — extends the thin landing state into a durable core tab
- P1.5 (portfolio/watchlist basics) — reuse quote-row hydration shape

## Considerations / gotchas

- Do not embed provider-specific fetch logic here. Quote comes from P1.1's provider-neutral contract.
- The landing state is intentionally thin — resist the urge to add earnings, holders, or peer modules. That's P1.3.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `app/shell/SymbolSearch.tsx`
- `app/symbol/QuoteSnapshot.tsx`
- `app/shell/SubjectDetailShell.tsx` (landing slot)
close_reason: Completed all P0.4 child work: shared symbol search/typeahead, resolver handoff into symbol detail, P1.1 quote stub, and first quote snapshot landing. Verified with web tests, typecheck, lint, and production build.

title: P0.4b Manual watchlist management baseline
description:
# P0.4b — Manual watchlist management baseline

## What

One implicit default manual watchlist as the baseline saved-subject model, with membership-only CRUD: view current members, add a resolved subject, remove a saved subject. Membership is canonical subject identity, not ticker strings.

## Why

Watchlists are the foundation of every saved-universe concept: dynamic watchlists (P4.7), agent universes (P5.1), screens (P1.4), themes (P4.1), and portfolio overlays (P1.5b) all build on top of a simple, user-owned collection. Getting the baseline membership contract right here prevents a sprawl of subject-collection systems later.

## Contract / acceptance

- Baseline CRUD is membership-only: view, add, remove.
- This bead does not define create-list, rename-list, delete-list, sharing, reordering, or multiple manual lists.
- The persisted membership unit is canonical subject identity — never a raw ticker string or stored quote payload.
- Membership is idempotent at subject level (adding the same canonical subject twice does not create duplicates).
- Manual watchlist rows hydrate quote context on read from the saved subject identity.
- Row hydration contract is lightweight: subject display identity, listing-sensitive symbol context, latest price, absolute move, percentage move, freshness/session state.
- Quote row hydration reuses P0.4's listing-oriented market identity rule — not a watchlist-specific quote model.
- Add-to-watchlist from public subject routes uses the inline auth interrupt from P0.1 (preserves route + pending action across sign-in).
- Removing a member does not mutate the underlying subject, quote snapshot, or portfolio overlay state.

## Depends on

- P0.4 (reusable search entry + selected-subject handoff)
- P0.1 (inline auth interrupt contract)

## Downstream consumers

- P1.5 (portfolio & watchlist basics) — builds on this baseline
- P4.7 (dynamic watchlists & overlays) — extends baseline with derivation modes
- P5.1 (agent CRUD) — may target user-owned collections without inventing a separate system

## Considerations / gotchas

- Storing ticker strings instead of `SubjectRef`s seems easier but breaks on cross-listing and restatement. Always persist the resolved canonical id.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `app/watchlists/ManualWatchlist.tsx`
- `/v1/watchlists/*` handlers
- `watchlists`, `watchlist_members` tables (already in schema pack)
close_reason: All three children closed: fra-6al.6.1 (default manual watchlist + membership CRUD), fra-6al.6.2 (row quote hydration via shared listing-oriented snapshot), fra-6al.6.3 (add-to-watchlist with inline auth interrupt). Note: row quote hydration was migrated from the P0.4 stub to the real market service in fra-cw0.1.6 (each row now fetches via /v1/market/quote).

title: P0.1 App shell & auth session/gating
description:
# P0.1 — App shell & auth session/gating

## What

One persistent workspace shell that owns three regions: left navigation, main canvas, right-rail slot. Auth is *in-shell*: unauthenticated users may browse public routes (Home, Screener, Analyze-entry, symbol detail). Protected routes (Chat, Agents, watchlists) render an in-shell auth gate instead of redirecting to a separate auth page.

## Why

The shell is the durable navigation frame regardless of auth state. Putting auth on the shell instead of the routes means session loss degrades gracefully (protected panels re-gate in place; public content keeps rendering). This contract is why Chat can live in the same shell as public Home without Home becoming session-scoped.

## Contract / acceptance

- Left navigation contains `Home`, `Agents`, `Chat`, `Screener`, `Analyze`.
- Shell chrome persists across all primary-workspace transitions.
- Unauthenticated users can enter the shell and navigate to public surfaces.
- Protected workspaces (`Chat`, `Agents`, user-owned watchlists, persisted Analyze runs) use soft in-shell guards; they do not redirect.
- Public routes may launch protected actions via inline auth interrupts (e.g., `save to watchlist`) that preserve the current route + pending-action payload so sign-in resumes the action.
- Session expiry inside a protected surface collapses protected content to the in-shell auth gate; public surfaces keep rendering.
- The shell is *not* auth-gated as a whole.

## Depends on

- None (foundation work).

## Downstream consumers

- P0.2 Route skeleton (lives inside this shell).
- P0.4 Symbol search (uses shell-owned search entry).
- P2.1 Thread coordinator (Chat is a primary workspace in this shell).
- P4.5 Right-rail activity (uses shell-owned right-rail slot).

## Considerations / gotchas

- Auth backend is intentionally abstract here — the contract is written in terms of "authenticated session scope" rather than a specific identity provider. Don't over-specify the identity backend in this bead.
- The right-rail slot is *shell-owned*, not surface-owned. Screener defaults to a denser main-canvas layout without using the rail; it may opt in later without changing the shell contract.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `app/shell/WorkspaceShell.tsx` (or framework equivalent)
- `app/shell/AuthGate.tsx`
- `app/shell/RightRailSlot.tsx`
- Auth session middleware / route guard
close_reason: P0.1 App shell complete. Four original sub-beads shipped: fra-6al.1.1 (persistent workspace shell), fra-6al.1.2 (in-shell auth gate), fra-6al.1.3 (inline auth interrupt), fra-6al.1.4 (light/dark parity). IA amendment fra-4pz realigned the shell to the video target: left rail = WatchlistSlot (P0.4b fills), TopBar + PrimaryTabs above main canvas, right rail unchanged. All acceptance criteria met: shell persists across navigations, public routes work unauthed, protected routes gate in-place, session loss collapses only protected content, public routes can fire inline auth interrupts, theme toggle flips without remount.

title: P0.2 Route skeleton & right-rail slot
description:
# P0.2 — Route skeleton & right-rail slot

## What

Nested-route structure matching the spec's route model: primary workspace routes (`/home`, `/agents`, `/chat/:threadId`, `/screener`, `/analyze`) plus an entered subject-detail shell (`/symbol/:subjectRef/{overview|financials|earnings|holders|signals}`). These are app navigation route groups, *not* `/v1/*` HTTP endpoint groups.

## Why

Nested routes are the durable model for subject-detail sections because the subject-detail shell must own shared subject header context and local section navigation while the outer workspace shell persists. Conflating symbol routes into a top-level workspace breaks the "entered subject" model the videos show.

## Contract / acceptance

- `Chat` is thread-scoped, not symbol-scoped (threads may span themes, multiple subjects, or imported Analyze artifacts).
- `Analyze` is a top-level workspace (not only a symbol-detail tab), but it accepts deep-linked `SubjectRef` context from symbol detail.
- `Screener` remains a primary workspace for building + viewing one active screen plus results.
- Symbol detail is an entered route group keyed by canonical subject identity — entering swaps the main canvas into a subject-detail shell while preserving outer shell chrome.
- Initial durable subject-detail sections: `overview`, `financials`, `earnings`, `holders`, `signals`.
- `signals` is the extensible section for community, sentiment, news pulse, future alt-data — not a source-specific `/reddit` route.

## Depends on

- P0.1 (app shell must exist before routes can live inside it).

## Downstream consumers

- P0.4, P1.3, P1.3b, P1.4b, P2.1, P4.2, P4.4, P4.5 all depend on this route skeleton.

## Considerations / gotchas

- Do not add a `/symbol/:ticker/*` route. Use the canonical `SubjectRef` identity so dual-class, ADR, and cross-listing cases work.
- `Analyze` accepting deep-linked subject context is a *workspace transition*, not a symbol-detail tab expansion.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `app/routes/*` (framework router config)
- `app/shell/SubjectDetailShell.tsx`
notes: Nested protected routes should not be wrapped with <ProtectedSurface>. Declare route metadata via handle: { scope: 'protected', label? } on the parent route and let RouteScopeGate enforce the auth gate from the deepest matched handle; child routes inherit parent scope, so nested paths like /chat/:threadId do not need their own wrapper.
close_reason: P0.2 route skeleton complete. All three sub-beads shipped: fra-6al.2.1 (thread-scoped /chat + /chat/:threadId), fra-6al.2.2 (entered subject-detail shell at /symbol/:subjectRef with Overview/Financials/Earnings/Holders/Signals), fra-6al.2.3 (declarative route-level scope metadata + RouteScopeGate). Right-rail slot unchanged (still mechanism from fra-6al.1.1, made always-mounted in fra-4pz). Route skeleton downstream consumers (P0.4, P1.3, P1.3b, P1.4b, P2.1, P4.2, P4.4, P4.5) unblocked.

title: Epic PX — Cross-cutting (observability, tool runtime, frontend)
description:
# Epic PX — Cross-cutting

## Goal

Infrastructure that spans phases and cannot live inside one of them: observability and evals, the tool runtime (bundle selection, audience enforcement, approvals, budgets), and the frontend BlockRegistry renderer. These have to exist by the time P2 comes online, but they evolve through every phase.

## Child tasks

- PX.1 Observability — `tool_call_log`, `citation_log`, `verifier_fail_log`, `extraction_eval`, golden-set evals, drift report
- PX.2 Tool runtime & orchestration — registry loader, bundle selector, audience enforcement, cost-class budgets, approval interceptors, prompt-cache ordering
- PX.3 Frontend renderer — `BlockRegistry` keyed by block `kind`, streaming, virtualized lists, scroll tailing, skeleton-first charts, memoized blocks by content hash

## Exit criteria

- Every assistant turn produces `tool_call_log`, `citation_log`, and (if sealed) a snapshot row.
- `finance_research_tool_registry.json` is the single source of truth for tool metadata.
- Audience enforcement is automated: analyst-audience tools cannot receive raw document bodies under any prompt sequence.
- A block rendered in chat, Analyze, and a finding uses the same React component instance from the registry.
- Prompt-cache order is `tools → system → bundle policy → response schema → few-shots → thread summary → resolved subjects/period → user turn`.

## Invariants this epic primarily guards

- **I4** — PX.2 is the code path that enforces "analyst never sees raw text."
- **I7** — PX.2 is the code path that intercepts write-intent tools into pending actions.

## Why cross-cutting

PX.1 observability is needed by every other epic (without it, evals can't catch regressions). PX.2 tool runtime is the engine inside P2's turn loop and P5's agent loop. PX.3 renderer is consumed by chat (P2.3), Home (P4.4), Analyze (P4.2), and findings (P5.3).

title: Epic P4 — Product parity layers
description:
# Epic P4 — Product parity layers

## Goal

Close the gap between "working research product" and "feels like the videos": themes as first-class subjects, Analyze as a saved template workflow, the shared artifact flow ("Add results to chat"), the findings-first Home feed with cluster dedupe, the right-rail activity stream, specialized Reddit/news blocks, and dynamic watchlists / portfolio overlays.

## Child tasks

- P4.1 Themes & macro subjects (Theme, ThemeMembership, theme chats)
- P4.2 Analyze template system (CRUD + `analyze_template_run` bundle + memo layout)
- P4.3 Shared artifact flow (add Analyze memo into chat thread with shared snapshot)
- P4.4 Home feed ranking & cluster dedupe (cross-agent findings)
- P4.5 Right-rail activity stream (`Reading` / `Investigating` / `Found` / `Dismissed`)
- P4.6 Specialized social & news blocks (`news_cluster`, `sentiment_trend`, `mention_volume`)
- P4.7 Dynamic watchlists & portfolio overlays (`mode: manual|screen|agent|theme|portfolio`)

## Exit criteria

- A thread titled "Iran-US Trade Tensions" works the same as a ticker thread but anchors on a `theme` subject.
- Analyze template runs produce a memo that can be "Added to chat" as a referenced artifact (same snapshot) without re-running the analyst.
- Home feed is cluster-deduped so the same story across three outlets shows as one card.
- Right-rail activity is distinct from findings (findings are durable product; activity is execution telemetry).
- A Reddit thread in `signals` renders via shared evidence-backed blocks, never raw social text panes.
- A screen-derived dynamic watchlist regenerates membership from the saved screen definition, not a frozen row cache.

## Invariants this epic primarily guards

- **I2** / **I3** — specialized social blocks still bind to evidence-backed claims, not raw text.
- **I5** — shared artifact flow reuses sealed snapshots; add-to-chat does not re-seal.

## Why this ordering

Without P4 the app works but doesn't *look* like the videos. Home-feed dedupe only makes sense after P3's `ClaimCluster` exists. Analyze templates only make sense after P2.3 block registry and P2.4 snapshot contract exist. Shared artifact flow only works once snapshots are the sealed handoff unit.

title: Epic P5 — Background agents
description:
# Epic P5 — Background agents

## Goal

Put thesis agents behind the Home feed: agent CRUD with thesis + universe + cadence + alert rules + watermarks; a queue runner with idempotent runs; finding generation with severity scoring; alert evaluation with approval gating; and notification channels (push/email/SMS/digest). Agents run reader extraction over deltas, update clusters, run analyst on relevant evidence, and emit `Finding` + `RunActivity`.

## Child tasks

- P5.1 Agent CRUD, scheduling, watermark storage (transactional advance)
- P5.2 Queue runner & idempotent agent runs (Cloudflare Queues are at-least-once)
- P5.3 Finding generation & severity scoring (sealed snapshot per finding)
- P5.4 Alert evaluation & approval gating (`create_alert` pending-action flow)
- P5.5 Notification channels — web push, email, SMS, mobile push, digest

## Exit criteria

- An agent with cadence `daily` and a thesis about AI chips produces at least one finding after a filing drop.
- Re-running the same queue message does not duplicate findings or double-advance watermarks.
- A `create_alert` tool call from chat returns a pending-action id; nothing fires until the user approves.
- Findings dedupe via `ClaimCluster` on the Home feed.
- `RunActivity` rows emit with stages `reading | investigating | found | dismissed` and drive the right-rail.

## Invariants this epic primarily guards

- **I7** (side effects require approval) — alerts and agent creation.
- **I4** (analyst never sees raw text) — agent loop still uses reader for extraction.

## Why this ordering

Agents are the most operationally complex subsystem. They require P3 (evidence plane), P2.4 (snapshot assembler), and PX.2 (approval flow) to already exist. Putting agents last keeps the early graph debuggable.

## Gotchas

- Cloudflare Queues provide at-least-once delivery and do NOT guarantee publish order. Runs must be idempotent. Watermark advance must not assume FIFO.
- Low-confidence extractions must land in a reviewer queue (P6.3), not silently auto-promote.

title: Epic P6 — Hard cases & scale
description:
# Epic P6 — Hard cases & scale

## Goal

Harden the coverage and operational footprint: segment extraction refinement (issuer-specific extension facts and non-GAAP logic), non-US identity/data/coverage expansion, a reviewer queue for low-confidence extractions, the export/share policy (what private overlay state travels with a shared artifact), and scale hardening (cache layout, freshness audits, provider bottleneck investigation).

## Child tasks

- P6.1 Segment extraction refinement (XBRL extensions, non-GAAP, axis evolution)
- P6.2 Non-US identity/data/coverage gaps (beyond SEC EDGAR)
- P6.3 Reviewer queues for low-confidence extractions and disputed promotions
- P6.4 Export & share policy (overlay state, entitlement channels)
- P6.5 Scale hardening (cache identity audits, provider bottlenecks, snapshot storage)

## Exit criteria

- Segment facts survive segment redefinitions with explicit `definition_as_of` pinning.
- Non-US issuers resolve to canonical identity (LEI, ISIN) and at least one non-US exchange is first-class.
- Extraction failures with confidence below threshold enter reviewer queue rather than auto-promoting.
- Shared artifacts (chat messages, Analyze memos) respect `entitlement_channels` per fact.
- Cache hit rate is monitored; cache identity (subject × range × interval × basis × normalization × freshness boundary) is auditable.

## Why this is last

P6 is ambient hardening. Its work applies to data and systems already operational. Scheduling it earlier either blocks on pieces that don't exist yet or produces hardening that must be redone once shapes change.

## Invariants this epic primarily guards

- **I1** / **I3** — promotion rules stay honest at scale; reviewer queue catches ambiguous cases.
- **I7** — export/share respects entitlement channels (some facts are not exportable).

title: Epic P2 — Structured chat & snapshots
description:
# Epic P2 — Structured chat & snapshots

## Goal

Build the interactive research chat: a Durable-Object-style thread coordinator, SSE transport, a pre-resolve router that runs before the analyst, the block registry, the snapshot assembler, and the verifier that enforces I1–I5 at seal time. Assistant output is strictly `Block[]`, never markdown. Every response binds to a sealed `SnapshotManifest`. Old messages remain interactive within the snapshot contract.

## Child tasks

- P2.1 Thread coordinator & SSE transport (session-scoped, per-thread ordering)
- P2.2 Pre-resolve router & budget policy (deterministic, before the analyst loop)
- P2.3 Block registry versioning & initial block catalog
- P2.4 Snapshot assembler & verifier (staging → verify → seal → transform-legality)

## Exit criteria

- Assistant responses validate against `spec/finance_research_block_schema.json` or fail the verifier.
- Every numeric reference in `rich_text` segments resolves to a fact/claim/event id present in the snapshot.
- `/v1/snapshots/{id}/transform` returns 200 for legal in-snapshot transforms and 409 (refresh required) for out-of-snapshot intents.
- Changing a chart range from YTD to 1Y inside an old message works without a refresh, provided the range end ≤ `as_of`.
- SSE events include at minimum: `turn.started`, `tool.started`, `tool.completed`, `snapshot.staged`, `snapshot.sealed`, `block.began`, `block.delta`, `block.completed`, `turn.completed`, `turn.error`.

## Invariants this epic primarily guards

- **I1** (backing row for every number) — verifier rejects responses with orphan refs.
- **I2** (narrative matches visuals) — `rich_text` segments bind fact/claim/event ids resolved from the same snapshot as charts.
- **I5** (answer pinned to a moment) — sealed snapshot manifest is the pin.
- **I8** (explicit refresh) — `allowed_transforms` + 409 boundary.
- **I7** (approvals) — verifier enforces approval gating for pending actions.

## Why this ordering

Without P2 the app is a terminal, not a research product. P2 is also where the prompt-cache ordering (`tools → system → messages`) and structured-output discipline get locked in. P3 (evidence plane) feeds the analyst through PX.2 reader tools, but without the verifier/snapshot plumbing, evidence-backed answers have no place to be pinned. P4 and P5 all render through the same block registry and rely on the snapshot contract.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic P3 — Document & evidence plane
description:
# Epic P3 — Document & evidence plane

## Goal

Stand up the full document → claim → fact pipeline: source/document models with object storage for raw blobs, a reader extraction path that is the *only* code allowed to see raw untrusted text, the claim/event/impact graph with clustering, promotion rules for candidate facts, and the evidence-bundle assembler used by analyst-facing `get_evidence_bundle`.

## Child tasks

- P3.1 Source & document models + object storage (R2/S3) + content-hash dedupe
- P3.2 Document ingestion & reader extraction (SEC EDGAR, press, transcripts, news, X, Reddit, uploads)
- P3.3 Entity linking & mentions (reader-side subject linking)
- P3.4 Claim / event / impact graph & claim clustering (`ClaimCluster`, `ClaimClusterMember`)
- P3.5 Promotion rules for candidate facts → Fact with supersession
- P3.6 Evidence-bundle assembler (`build_evidence_bundle`, `get_evidence_bundle`)

## Exit criteria

- Analyst-facing code paths cannot read raw document bytes (enforced by tool audience).
- Documents with identical `content_hash` dedupe to one row.
- Claims carry `modality`, `attributed_to_type`, `effective_time`, `confidence`, `status`.
- `EntityImpact` edges route claims to subjects with `direction`, `channel`, `horizon`.
- Candidate facts from non-primary sources never silently become authoritative.
- A filing restatement supersedes the prior fact rather than overwriting it.
- `ClaimCluster` dedupes similar claims and powers Home-feed cluster dedupe.

## Invariants this epic primarily guards

- **I3** (documents are evidence, not truth)
- **I4** (analyst never sees raw untrusted text)
- **I6** (multi-entity reasoning via claims + impacts, not mention counts)

## Why this ordering

P4 (Home feed, Analyze with source categories, specialized social/news blocks) all assume the evidence plane exists. P5 (agents) runs reader extraction over deltas. Without P3 complete, agents have nothing to find, Home has nothing to rank, and Analyze templates cannot cite evidence.

title: Epic P1 — Terminal core
description:
# Epic P1 — Terminal core

## Goal

Build the deterministic research terminal layer: provider-neutral market data, fundamentals (statement normalization + stats/segment/consensus aggregation), symbol detail surfaces (overview, financials, earnings, holders, signals), the screener query model + surface, and portfolio/watchlist basics. At the end of P1, the app is usable as a read-only finance terminal even without chat.

## Child tasks

- P1.1 Market data service (quote/bar provider abstraction)
- P1.1b Adjusted series query & caching semantics
- P1.2 Fundamentals service (statement & metric normalization)
- P1.2b Stats, segment, and consensus aggregations
- P1.3 Symbol detail — overview / financials / earnings core tabs
- P1.3b Symbol detail — holders, signals, Analyze entry integration
- P1.4 Screener query model & result contract
- P1.4b Screener surface flow & saved-screen handoff
- P1.5 Portfolio holdings model & overlay inputs
- P1.5b Portfolio & watchlist first surface behaviors

## Exit criteria

- Quote and bar retrieval are provider-agnostic: swapping providers requires no downstream changes.
- Series responses carry explicit `basis`, `normalization`, `as_of`, `coverage_start`, `source_id`.
- Statement tables never silently mix `as_reported` and `as_restated`.
- Segment facts expose `axis`, `definitions`, and `coverage_warnings` (never flattened into issuer-level tables).
- Screener runs publicly; saving a screen is gated to session.
- Watchlist membership is idempotent at canonical subject level (not ticker).
- Symbol detail navigation preserves subject context and shell chrome across tab transitions.

## Invariants this epic primarily guards

- **I1** (no displayed number without a backing Fact/Computation row) — enforced by fundamentals + market data services funneling through Fact/Computation.
- **I2** (narrative and visuals must agree) — metric/segment aggregations are the value layer that chat + blocks will reference by id.
- Identity discipline — downstream modules only consume resolved subject refs, never raw tickers.

## Why this ordering

P2 (chat) depends on `get_series`, `get_statement_facts`, `get_key_stats`, `get_segment_facts`, `get_analyst_consensus`, `get_eps_surprise`, `get_peer_set` already returning canonical facts with provenance. Without P1 complete, the analyst tool loop would either have to invent a shadow data layer or return ungrounded numbers — both invariant violations. P4 (Home, Analyze) and P5 (agents) layer narrative atop the same terminal-core services.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic P0 — Foundation & shell
description:
# Epic P0 — Foundation & shell

## Goal

Stand up the durable scaffolding everything else plugs into: the persistent workspace shell, auth session model, route skeleton, identity resolver service, symbol search, first quote snapshot, manual watchlist baseline, relational schema (from `spec/finance_research_db_schema.sql`), and the observability skeleton. At the end of P0 a user can open the app (authenticated or not), search for a ticker, land on a subject-detail shell showing a live quote, and (if authenticated) save it to a default watchlist.

## Child tasks

- P0.1 App shell & auth session/gating
- P0.2 Route skeleton & right-rail slot
- P0.3 Identity & resolver service
- P0.3b Search-to-subject resolution flow
- P0.4 Symbol search & first quote snapshot surface
- P0.4b Manual watchlist management baseline
- P0.5 DB bootstrap (apply schema pack + migrations)
- P0.6 Dev infra & observability skeleton

## Exit criteria

- `bd ready` from P1 tasks becomes populated (i.e., their P0 blockers are closed).
- User can navigate Home / Screener / Analyze-entry / Symbol-detail without a session.
- User can search "AAPL", pick among resolver candidates, and land on the subject-detail shell with a freshness-stamped quote.
- Authenticated user can add that subject to the default manual watchlist.
- `/v1/subjects/resolve` returns `resolved | ambiguous | not_found` envelopes.
- Database has applied `spec/finance_research_db_schema.sql` and tables are populated with reference data.

## Invariants this epic primarily guards

- **I5** (answers pinned to a moment) is seeded here via the listing-oriented quote snapshot with `as_of`.
- Canonical identity discipline (issuer vs instrument vs listing; ticker is a locator).

## Why this ordering

Everything downstream assumes the resolver returns canonical `SubjectRef`s rather than raw ticker strings, that the shell owns navigation and right-rail slots (so P4.5 activity and P2.1 chat can plug in), and that the schema is applied (so P1.* services have tables to query). Skipping any P0 piece forces shortcuts that become invariant violations later (e.g., P1.1 calling a provider with a ticker string instead of a listing id).
close_reason: All P0 exit criteria satisfied via closed children: app shell + auth gating (.1), route skeleton (.2), resolver service (.3), search-to-subject flow (.4), symbol search + quote snapshot (.5), manual watchlist baseline (.6), DB bootstrap (.7), dev infra + observability skeleton (.8). P1 work (fra-cw0.* market data service) is unblocked and in flight.

title: Fix isPoolLike brand check in snapshot-sealer + chat-messages (rejects valid pg.PoolClient)
description:
From fra-dpj implementation: the transaction-client brand check at services/snapshot/src/snapshot-sealer.ts:240-244 and services/chat/src/messages.ts:219-223 has a latent bug — pg.PoolClient inherits .connect() from pg.Client AND adds .release(), so 'has .connect = function' returns true for a valid acquired pool client. The brand throws 'use the WithPool variant for pools' for callers that DID use the WithPool variant correctly, blocking integration with real pg pools.

Symptom: anyone calling sealSnapshotWithPool(realPgPool, ...) or persistChatMessageAfterSnapshotSealWithPool(realPgPool, ...) gets the error even though they did exactly what the API asked for. Existing unit tests pass because the recordingDb fixture lacks .connect; no integration test exercises the real-pg path.

Fix (mirroring the fra-dpj fix at services/analyze/src/template-runner.ts):
- isPoolLike: distinguish Pool (has .connect, no .release) from PoolClient (has both)
- isAcquiredClient: unchanged (.release alone is sufficient)

Files:
- services/snapshot/src/snapshot-sealer.ts:240-249
- services/chat/src/messages.ts:219-228 (and any other modules that copy this pattern — grep for isPoolLike)

Add an integration test in each that exercises the *WithPool variant against a real pg pool to prevent the bug from coming back. db/test/docker-pg.ts now exports connectedPool() (added in fra-dpj) for exactly this.

title: fra-vme integration test for inferred theme membership SQL
description: Why: fra-vme code review flagged that the load-bearing SQL in services/themes/src/inferred-membership.ts (5 subqueries + UNION ALL + group by) is currently only stub-asserted via assert.match on query text. A typo in the CTE wiring or a wrong count(distinct ...) would not be caught. What: Add an integration test in services/themes/test/inferred-membership.integration.test.ts that uses the existing db/test/docker-pg.ts pattern (see services/chat/test/threads-repo.integration.test.ts), seeds a small fixture of (sources, documents, claims, claim_arguments, entity_impacts, claim_clusters, claim_cluster_members, themes), then asserts computeInferredThemeCandidates returns the expected rows in the documented order, with rationale_claim_ids populated. Acceptance: at least one happy-path test that exercises both claim_arguments and entity_impacts signal sources and verifies the support-only relation filter excludes contradictions.
close_reason: 4 integration tests against real Postgres, covering both signal-source merging, the support-only contradiction-exclusion contract, min_confidence + impact_directions filters, and applyInferredThemeMembership round-trip with rationale persistence and replay idempotency. All pass against postgres:15 via docker-pg.

title: theme_memberships: add unique index + ON CONFLICT addThemeMembership
description:
From simplify review of fra-2ju (PR #41-follow-up).

Two related schema/repo changes:

1. theme_memberships lacks a unique constraint on (theme_id, subject_kind, subject_id). The current addThemeMembership uses select-then-insert, which is racy under a connection pool: two concurrent adds may both observe empty and both insert, producing duplicate membership rows.

2. After the unique index lands, switch addThemeMembership in services/themes/src/theme-repo.ts to INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (theme_id, subject_kind, subject_id) DO NOTHING RETURNING ..., mirroring services/watchlists/src/queries.ts:79. One round trip on the create path (the hot one) instead of two.

3. While at it, add covering indexes for the membership list ORDER BY:
   - (theme_id, score DESC NULLS LAST, effective_at ASC)
   - (subject_kind, subject_id, score DESC NULLS LAST, effective_at ASC)
   The existing theme_memberships_theme_idx and theme_memberships_subject_idx cover the WHERE filter but not the sort, so a popular theme triggers an in-memory sort on every read.

Files:
- spec/finance_research_db_schema.sql (or a numbered migration if the project uses incremental migrations — check db/migrations/)
- services/themes/src/theme-repo.ts (switch to ON CONFLICT)
- services/themes/test/theme-repo.test.ts (drop the two-call existing-then-insert assertion in 'addThemeMembership inserts when ... returns status created'; assert single-call ON CONFLICT semantics instead)
close_reason: Closed addThemeMembership race via unique (theme_id, subject_kind, subject_id) + INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING RETURNING. Replaced single-column theme_memberships indexes with covering ones matching the list ORDER BY. Verified end-to-end with a 10-writer concurrent integration test. Reviewed: ready to merge. Commits: 9746b3f, f724224.

title: Implement S3-compatible ObjectStore adapter (R2/S3/MinIO)
description:
What: A wire adapter that satisfies the `ObjectStore` interface from services/evidence/src/object-store.ts against any S3-compatible API (Cloudflare R2, AWS S3, MinIO).

Why: fra-0v4 shipped the content-addressed contract and an in-memory implementation. P3.2 ingestion needs a remote backend before raw bytes can durably leave the dev process. The interface is finalized so this is a thin adapter, not a redesign.

Contract:
- New file services/evidence/src/s3-object-store.ts implements `ObjectStore` (put/get/has) over an injected S3 client + bucket + optional key prefix.
- put: HEAD first, return already_present if 200; else PutObject. Key = sanitized raw_blob_id (drop the `sha256:` prefix or namespace under it).
- get: GetObject, stream body to Uint8Array, return null on 404.
- has: HeadObject, true on 200, false on 404; rethrow on other errors.
- Use @aws-sdk/client-s3 (the standard for R2/S3/MinIO; first new SDK dep in services/).
- Adds @aws-sdk/client-s3 to services/evidence/package.json dependencies.
- Add an env block to .env.dev.example: BLOB_STORE_BACKEND={memory|s3}, S3_ENDPOINT, S3_REGION, S3_BUCKET, S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID, S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, S3_FORCE_PATH_STYLE.
- Optional: add a minio service to docker-compose.dev.yml so integration tests can hit a real S3 API.

Verification:
- Unit tests with a mocked S3 client cover put created/already_present, get hit/miss, has true/false, and 4xx propagation.
- Integration test (gated on a MinIO container the way db tests gate on Docker) round-trips bytes through put → get and asserts content-addressed key derivation matches HEAD.

Once this lands, parent fra-6j0.1 (P3.1) can close.
close_reason: Implemented S3-compatible ObjectStore wire adapter in services/evidence/src/s3-object-store.ts. put HEADs first and short-circuits to already_present on hit; otherwise uploads with ChecksumSHA256 for server-side body verification. get/has translate 404 to null/false; non-404 errors propagate. Keys namespaced as {keyPrefix?}/sha256/{hex} for future hash-family extensibility. First @aws-sdk/client-s3 dependency in services/. .env.dev.example documents BLOB_STORE_BACKEND=memory|s3 plus S3_*; docker-compose.dev.yml gains a MinIO service. Verified with services/evidence npm test (50 pass: 14 unit with mocked S3 client + 4 real-MinIO integration via new bootstrapMinio harness + 32 pre-existing). Code review caught isNotFoundError trusting httpStatusCode too broadly (would mask R2's AccessDenied-as-404), FIFO t.after ordering risk, and MinIO image pinning; all addressed in commit 8056278.

title: Tighten analyst tool schemas against raw payload fields
description: Follow-up from fra-cms code review. The audience runtime now blocks actual raw document fields in analyst call arguments and results, but many analyst registry schemas still use broad additionalProperties placeholders. Tighten those schemas so static validation can prove raw document handles cannot hide inside analyst-facing payload shapes without collapsing legitimate structured claim/fact/event objects.
acceptance_criteria: Analyst-facing input/output schemas either use closed explicit properties or explicit sanitized ref/value object shapes; static audience-boundary validation has adversarial tests for permissive analyst schemas; default registry passes the stricter validation.
close_reason: Tightened analyst registry schemas by replacing analyst-side permissive additionalProperties with raw-key-guarded analyst_safe_json_value refs, and extended static audience validation/tests to reject permissive or unguarded analyst schema maps.

title: Implement screener executor + /v1/screener HTTP handlers
description:
What: Service-side execution of validated ScreenerQueries plus the /v1/screener/* HTTP boundary. Wraps the contract substrate landed in cw0.7.1-3 with the actual runtime: enumerate universe candidates, hydrate market + fundamentals values via existing service interfaces, apply filter clauses, rank by sort spec, apply page offset/limit, and assemble the ScreenerResultRow envelope. Plus /v1/screener/search (run a query) and the supporting /v1/screener/screens endpoints (save / list / open / delete persisted ScreenSubjects, with replay routed through the executor for fresh data).

Why: cw0.7.1-3 land the contract substrate (envelope, row, persistable subject) but the parent epic explicitly requires "Service owns query validation, execution, ranking, pagination, row-envelope assembly" and "/v1/screener/* is the client boundary." Without this bead, no client can actually run a screen — they would have to fan out to /v1/market/* + /v1/fundamentals/* and invent their own join semantics, which is the spec violation the contract was written to prevent.

Contract:
- Executor consumes a normalized ScreenerQuery (cw0.7.1 output) and produces a ScreenerResponse (cw0.7.2 shape) where `rows.length <= page.limit`, rank is 1-based strictly increasing, and `total_count >= rows.length`.
- Executor reads market + fundamentals via the existing service interfaces (services/market, services/fundamentals); never fans out to provider payload columns.
- /v1/screener/search returns HTTP 200 with the structured ScreenerResponse envelope. Query validation errors return HTTP 400 with a structured rejection (matches the error envelope shape used by /v1/market/series and /v1/fundamentals/*).
- Saved-screen endpoints persist only ScreenSubject (cw0.7.3 shape) — never row payloads. Reopen routes replay through the executor for fresh data, never returns cached rows.
- LIMIT_MAX (500) cap from cw0.7.1 is enforced server-side; over-limit requests return 400.

Verification:
- Executor on AAPL+MSFT universe with `market_cap > $1T` returns both, ranked desc by market_cap.
- /v1/screener/search end-to-end test: POST a valid query → typed ScreenerResponse; POST a query with `polygon.lastTrade.p` field → structured 400 with "unknown screener field" message.
- Save → reopen → reissue round-trip: POST a screen, GET it back, POST /search with the replayed query — yields fresh rows with current quote/fundamentals timestamps (not the timestamps from save time).
- Files to touch (likely): services/screener/src/executor.ts, services/screener/src/ranker.ts, services/screener/src/screen-repository.ts, services/screener/src/http.ts, services/screener/src/dev.ts, services/screener/test/{executor,ranker,http}.test.ts, plus dev fixtures wiring AAPL + MSFT into the screener universe.
- Mirrors the contract structure of services/market and services/fundamentals: in-memory repository pattern, dev fixture loader, HTTP server skeleton consumed by services/dev-api proxy.
close_reason: Implemented screener executor (filter→sort→page→assemble), saved-screen repository, and HTTP server with all 7 endpoints. 16 new HTTP integration tests + 23 unit tests (executor + candidate registry) + 10 screen-repo tests added; all 115 tests pass. Verification target met: /v1/screener/screens/:id/replay re-runs through the executor on every call (test asserts different as_of stamps across two replays of the same screen). Stacked on PR #26 branch; ready for its own PR once that merges.

title: Expose GET /v1/fundamentals/earnings HTTP endpoint
description:
What: Stand up GET /v1/fundamentals/earnings on the fundamentals service. Define an EarningsEvents envelope (per-quarter chronology with release_date, fiscal_period, eps_actual, eps_estimate_at_release, surprise_pct), an EarningsEventsRepository keyed on issuer pre-built from dev fixtures, and a GET endpoint returning the envelope.

Why: Earnings tab (fra-cw0.5.3) needs the chronology of past releases with EPS surprise — this is event-shaped data, not derived (the at-time estimate is frozen at release, not recomputed). No aggregator function needed; pattern follows IssuerProfileRepository (fra-cw0.12) — repository wraps fixtures directly.

Contract:
- GET /v1/fundamentals/earnings?issuer_id=<uuid>
- Returns 200 with { earnings: EarningsEventsEnvelope } or 404 missing_coverage envelope on unknown issuer
- Envelope: { subject, family: 'earnings_events', currency, events: [{ release_date, fiscal_year, fiscal_period, period_end, eps_actual, eps_estimate_at_release, surprise_pct, surprise_direction, source_id, as_of }], as_of }
- Validators reject missing actual or zero estimate (surprise_pct undefined when estimate is null)
- Dev fixtures: at least Apple's last 8 quarters
close_reason: Closed

title: Expose GET /v1/fundamentals/consensus HTTP endpoint
description:
What: Stand up GET /v1/fundamentals/consensus on the fundamentals service. Cherry-pick the analyst-consensus aggregator from PR #23 (feat/fra-cw0-4-3-analyst-consensus). Wire a ConsensusRepository keyed on issuer that pre-runs buildAnalystConsensus from dev fixtures, and a single GET endpoint returning the AnalystConsensusEnvelope (rating distribution + price target + forward estimates + coverage warnings).

Why: Earnings tab (fra-cw0.5.3) needs current consensus. The aggregator already exists on a feature branch — same cherry-pick-then-wire pattern as fra-cw0.13 (key-stats) and fra-cw0.15 (segment-facts).

Contract:
- GET /v1/fundamentals/consensus?issuer_id=<uuid>
- Returns 200 with { consensus: AnalystConsensusEnvelope } or 404 missing_coverage envelope on unknown issuer
- Repository pre-validates + pre-freezes at construction; hot path is Map.get
- Dev fixtures: at least Apple with full consensus shape (ratings + target + 2 forward estimates)
close_reason: Closed

title: Expose POST /v1/fundamentals/segments HTTP endpoint
description: What: Add POST /v1/fundamentals/segments handler that wraps the segment-facts aggregator (PR #22, branch feat/fra-cw0-4-2-segment-facts) and returns the derivation-transparent envelope: axis + segment_definitions + per-segment facts + coverage_warnings. Why: P1.3 financials tab needs segment donut + trajectory. The segment-facts aggregator exists on a remote branch but no HTTP exposes it. Contract: pass coverage_warnings through unmodified (never fabricate coverage); preserve axis + segment_definitions in the wire envelope; surface basis/period explicitly. Verification: integration test asserting (a) full inputs produce a segment envelope with axis+definitions, (b) sparse inputs surface coverage_warnings unmodified, (c) unknown issuer returns structured 404.
notes: Cherry-pick the segment-facts aggregator commit from origin/feat/fra-cw0-4-2-segment-facts (mirror of how PR #21 was cherry-picked for stats). Repo abstraction follows StatsRepository pattern: SegmentsRepository.find(issuer_id) returns SegmentFactsEnvelope | null. Pre-compute envelopes at construction (per the simplify pass on stats). Frontend consumer is fra-cw0.5.2.
close_reason: Landed: POST /v1/fundamentals/segments handler in services/fundamentals/src/http.ts. Validates issuer SubjectRef + axis enum (business/geography) + period (parsePeriod) + basis enum. Returns single SegmentFactsEnvelope per (axis, period) request, or 404 missing_coverage when unavailable. Cherry-picked the segment-facts aggregator (PR #22, 4 commits squashed to latest state at 0847912) so this branch can wire HTTP without waiting for the PR to merge — drops out cleanly on rebase. 171 pass, 0 fail. title: Make pending auth-interrupt actions durable (serialize route + payload, survive redirects/reload)
description: Current AuthInterruptProvider (fra-6al.1.3) stores pending protected actions as in-memory closures. Design: define a named ProtectedActionType enum + typed payload per type + dispatch registry; persist { actionType, payload, returnTo: { pathname, search, hash } } to sessionStorage; on session null→truthy transition, rehydrate and dispatch. Must also handle: stale entries (TTL), payload schema versioning, unmounted consumer (dispatch into registry, not closure). Implementation is tightly coupled to real auth backend — do this as part of whichever bead wires the first real identity provider.
close_reason: Implemented durable auth-interrupt pending actions: versioned actionType/payload envelopes, route snapshots for return-to context, sessionStorage rehydration, TTL rejection for stale entries, and updated resume consumers to dispatch typed payloads. title: PX.4 Title & summary async model (thread titles, finding summaries, Home headlines)
description:
# PX.4 — Title & summary async model

## What

An async model pass that generates: chat thread titles, thread summaries (rolling compression for the prompt-cache prefix), finding summaries, and Home card headlines. This is the fifth model role called out by the spec (`§12.5 Summary/title model`).

## Why

Without this role, chats use "New chat" forever, thread summaries don't compress, Home cards show raw cluster text, and finding headlines are claim-text dumps. The videos show clean "Iran-US Trade Tensions" style thread titles and concise finding headlines — those are generated, not user-typed.

## Contract / acceptance

- Thread title generator runs async after first exchange; updates `chat_threads.title`.
- Thread summary rolls per N turns, replacing older turns in the cache prefix.
- Finding summary generator produces `findings.headline` from sealed snapshot + cluster.
- Home card headline generator produces one-line headline from finding + cluster context.
- All outputs validate against a title/summary schema (no blocks — these are plain text by spec).
- Cheap/fast model class (this is explicitly NOT the analyst).
- Failures fall back to raw first-N-chars rather than blocking the surface.

## Depends on

- P2.1 (thread coordinator — writes titles)
- P5.3 (findings — generates finding summaries)
- P4.4 (Home — consumes headlines)

## Downstream consumers

- Every user-facing title/headline surface.

## Considerations / gotchas

- Async failures must never block the user-facing turn. Title stays as placeholder until generation succeeds.
- Rolling summaries must be deterministic enough that the prompt-cache prefix stays stable — don't summarize on every turn.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/summary/TitleGenerator.ts`
- `services/summary/SummaryGenerator.ts`
- `services/summary/HeadlineGenerator.ts`

description: What: Async worker that reads first assistant response + user intent and writes a concise title to chat_threads.title. Why: 'New chat' forever is the failure mode. Contract: Title is <= 60 chars; single line; async failure falls back to placeholder. Verification: Create thread; after first turn, title is human-readable.

Verification: Each block validates + renders correctly for sample inputs.

title: Implement corporate actions handling (splits, dividends, spin-offs)

close_reason: Added CorporateAction union (Split | CashDividend | StockDividend | SpinOff) with effective_date and source_id, smart constructor with per-kind validation, and applyCorporateActions(bars, actions, basis) that walks raw bars to a target AdjustmentBasis. Splits + stock dividends adjust price + volume; cash dividends + spin-offs scale pre-ex prices via the prevClose factor leaving volume untouched. Oldest-first action ordering composes correctly (verified across compounded splits and split+dividend mixes). Verification clause covered: 16 dedicated tests, including continuity-across-split-date (max day-to-day jump <= 1 unit after adjustment, vs >= 50 unit drop in unadjusted). 73 tests pass total.

Backend: services/market/src/http.ts exposes GET /v1/market/quote that goes through the polygon adapter seam, with a dev-mode fixture fetcher (services/market/src/dev-fixtures.ts) and an in-memory listing repo (services/market/src/listings.ts). title: Implement light/dark mode parity with theme toggle
description: What: Theme tokens + toggle wired across the persistent shell and all surfaces. Why: Spec calls out light/dark parity as a first-class UX requirement visible in videos. Contract: All shell + surface components respect theme tokens; toggle persists per user. Verification: Toggle theme in one surface; all surfaces update without remount.
close_reason: Implemented light/dark parity with three-mode (Light/Dark/Auto) segmented toggle. Tokens applied across shell (WorkspaceShell, LeftNav, SessionControl, RightRailSlot, AuthGate, AuthInterrupt, ThemeToggle) and all five pages. Only pre-existing RightRailSlot lint warning remains.

title: Implement virtualized message list + memoized blocks

close_reason: Implemented computeVirtualWindow (pure O(N) windowing math; perf test confirms <500µs/call at 1000 items, well under the 16.67ms/frame budget), VirtualizedMessageList (rAF-batched scroll, ResizeObserver-measured variable-height items, constant rendered count regardless of total length), MemoizedBlockView (React.memo + reference-equality comparator since snapshot blocks are immutable), MessageItem (memoized + content_hash as React key for cross-snapshot identity). Exported via web/src/chat/index.ts. Tests 232/232 (+12), typecheck and lint clean.

title: Implement scroll tailing + jump-to-latest affordance
description: What: Auto-tail new messages; jump button when user scrolled up. Why: Classic chat UX. Contract: Tailing respects user scroll position. Verification: Scroll up; new message appears; jump button shows.

Tests 238/238 (+6 scroll-tailing predicate cases: exact bottom, within threshold, past threshold, no-overflow case, custom threshold, threshold-positive sanity). Typecheck and lint clean.

Drift-detection test covers schema enum ↔ typed kind groups ↔ register helpers ↔ composer. Web tests 220/220, typecheck and lint clean.

title: Implement SSE-driven incremental block rendering
