title: P6.3 Reviewer queues for low-confidence extractions
description:
# P6.3 — Reviewer queues for low-confidence extractions

## What

Human-in-the-loop queue for extractions below the confidence threshold: low-confidence candidate facts, disputed claims, ambiguous entity links. Reviewers can approve, reject, or edit before promotion.

## Why

Silent auto-promotion of low-confidence extractions violates I3. A reviewer queue is the escape hatch that preserves promotion discipline without either dropping signal or blocking the whole pipeline.

## Contract / acceptance

- Queue rows reference the source document + extracted candidate.
- Reviewers can approve (promote per rules), reject (mark as rejected), or edit (revise fields + re-promote).
- Queue aging is monitored — stale queue items surface to operators.
- Queue throughput is capped per-reviewer to prevent rubber-stamping.

## Depends on

- P3.5 (promotion rules + threshold config)

## Downstream consumers

- (operations leaf)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Reviewer bias: two reviewers reaching different conclusions is a signal the threshold is wrong, not that one reviewer is right.

title: P6.4 Export & share policy
description:
# P6.4 — Export & share policy

## What

Policy framework controlling what travels when a user exports or shares an artifact (chat message, Analyze memo, finding): which facts may egress via which channels (`entitlement_channels[]`), which private overlay state (watchlist/held badges) travels with shared artifacts, and how disclosures are preserved.

## Why

Shared artifacts can leave the app boundary. Without explicit policy, entitlements get violated (delayed pricing emailed out) and private overlay state leaks (user A shares a memo showing user A's portfolio held-state).

## Contract / acceptance

- Every fact carries `entitlement_channels[]`; export/share respects it.
- Private overlay state (watchlist/held badges, portfolio quantities) is stripped by default from shared artifacts.
- Disclosures are preserved on exported artifacts (delayed pricing, candidate-vs-authoritative, etc.).
- Share URL artifacts resolve via sealed `snapshot_id` — the recipient sees the same evidence the author saw.

## Depends on

- P1.5b (user-owned state distinct from canonical content)
- P4.3 (shared artifact flow)

## Downstream consumers

- (leaf)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Print/PDF export must honor the same policy — not just web share.

title: P6.5 Scale hardening
description:
# P6.5 — Scale hardening

## What

Ambient performance work at scale: cache identity audits (P1.1b cache layout), provider bottleneck investigation, snapshot storage cost, tool-call-log retention, query plan audits on `facts` / `claims` / `snapshots`, and read-replica topology.

## Why

P6.5 applies to systems already operational. Doing it earlier produces hardening against a moving target.

## Contract / acceptance

- Cache hit rate is monitored; cache identity (P1.1b) is auditable via logs.
- Provider bottleneck audits identify slow/rate-limited vendors and produce fallback plans.
- Snapshot storage growth rate is tracked; cold snapshots move to archival storage.
- Tool call log retention policy documented; older rows archive or summarize.
- Query plan audits on partitioned tables confirm index usage.

## Depends on

- P1.1 (provider-neutral boundary)
- P1.1b (cache identity rules)

## Downstream consumers

- (operations leaf)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Don't optimize before measuring. Baselines first.

title: P6.1 Segment extraction refinement
description:
# P6.1 — Segment extraction refinement

## What

Beyond the SEC companyfacts aggregates: extraction of segment data, issuer-specific XBRL extension facts, non-GAAP reconciliations, and segment-axis evolution across filings. Preserves axis, definitions, and coverage warnings.

## Why

The SEC `data.sec.gov` companyfacts API explicitly excludes segment data, issuer-specific extension facts, and non-GAAP logic. A naive companyfacts-only implementation will ship financials tables that look complete but are missing the segment breakdowns users expect — the exact surface shown in the videos.

## Contract / acceptance

- Segment extraction reads XBRL with extension support.
- Segment-axis evolution across filings preserves `definition_as_of` pinning so segment redefinitions are visible.
- Non-GAAP reconciliations extract with explicit GAAP-to-non-GAAP mapping.
- Coverage warnings surface on segment breakdowns — e.g., "segment definition changed Q2 2024".
- Extensions extractor operates in reader audience (raw XBRL text may contain narrative).

## Depends on

- P1.2b (segment aggregation API)
- P3.2 (extraction pipeline)

## Downstream consumers

- P1.3 (financials tab segment renderers)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Some issuers redefine segments multiple times — historical comparability requires transparent handling.

title: P6.2 Non-US identity, data, and coverage gaps
description:
# P6.2 — Non-US identity, data, and coverage gaps

## What

Expand beyond US SEC EDGAR: non-US issuer identity (LEI, non-US ISINs, local identifiers), non-US exchange listings, local accounting standards (IFRS mapping in `metrics.canonical_source_class`), and provider/vendor coverage for international markets.

## Why

The videos focus on US markets, but the identity layer already supports `issuer.domicile` and `listing.mic` for a reason. International coverage is ambient rather than a Phase 0 requirement — hence P6.

## Contract / acceptance

- Non-US issuer resolution works for at least one major international exchange (e.g., LSE, TSE).
- IFRS metric mappings preserve `canonical_source_class: 'ifrs'` distinct from GAAP.
- Listings in non-USD trading currencies render without silent FX conversion (I7 for implicit conversions).
- Coverage warnings surface when non-US data is thinner than US data for a comparison.

## Depends on

- P1.1b (basis/coverage rules)
- P1.2 (statement normalization)
- P1.2b (aggregation outputs)

## Downstream consumers

- (leaf)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Do NOT silently FX-convert when comparing US and non-US listings — make the currency visible or force explicit normalization.

close_reason: Implemented a registry-backed turn tool policy that composes system-selected bundle classification with per-cost-class budgets, preserves graceful unknown-bundle rejection, returns the partial-answer budget path with explicit note on exhaustion, and stress-tests repeated high-cost calls while allowing other cost classes.

title: Implement subject extraction via resolver envelopes
description: What: Pre-resolve uses P0.3 resolver; ambiguity surfaces as a clarification response. Why: Never let the model silently resolve identity. Contract: Ambiguous inputs produce a clarification turn. Verification: User says 'GOOG' — system asks which share class when ambiguous.

title: Implement SSE reconnect with last-seq resume
description: What: Client can reconnect with Last-Event-ID and receive missed events. Why: Flaky networks must not lose assistant output. Contract: Reconnect yields events strictly after the resumed sequence. Verification: Simulate 5s disconnect mid-stream.
close_reason: SSE reconnect with Last-Event-ID was already implemented; the gap was verification. Added 3 deferred-gated async-runner tests that pin the bead's contract: (1) mid-stream client disconnect doesn't abort the turn — server-side emissions accumulate in coordinator history while no subscriber exists, and reconnect with Last-Event-ID:N replays them strictly in seq>N order; (2) turn keeps running through a disconnect — all 9 events emit server-side even when the client received only #1; (3) reconnect across a wall-clock gap with no in-flight events stays open until the runner produces more. Comments document microtask-drain bounds and the live-vs-history-replay paths per code review. 104 chat tests pass.

title: Persist chat_messages only after snapshot.sealed
description: What: Message persistence is gated on seal success. Why: Persisting before seal means invariant-violating messages become history. Contract: No chat_message row without snapshot_id. Verification: Induce verifier failure; confirm no message row.
close_reason: Delivered snapshot-sealed chat message persistence gate with migration/spec updates, runtime wiring, transaction pinning, and persistence/schema tests. Reviewed after implementation; chat/db tests passed with Docker-backed DB tests skipped where Docker unavailable.

title: Implement SSE event schema + sequence numbers
description: What: Normative event set with monotonically increasing sequence numbers and turn/block correlation. Why: Frontend reconnect must resume from last delivered sequence. Contract: All 10 event kinds carry turn_id + seq. Verification: Kill+resume SSE; last-seq reconnect completes stream.
close_reason: The SSE event schema, sequencer, correlation-field enforcement, and the 10-kind event set were all already implemented in services/chat/src/sse.ts. The gap was unit-test coverage of the bead's contract. Added services/chat/test/sse.test.ts with 10 focused tests pinning: (1) every one of the 10 event kinds carries seq + turn_id + thread_id + run_id + type via the sequencer; (2) turn_id defaults to run_id when not provided; (3) sequencer produces strictly monotonic seq across kind transitions; (4) payload fields cannot forge the universal fields (spread order); (5) block.* requires block_id, tool.* requires tool_call_id, snapshot.* requires snapshot_id, all rejecting whitespace-only ids; (6) turn.* requires no extra correlation field; (7) createChatSseEvent rejects 0/negative/fractional/NaN/Infinity for seq; (8) stubSuccessEvents returns the 9-event canonical success stream with turn.error reserved for the failure path. Bead's verification step (Kill+resume SSE; last-seq reconnect completes stream) is satisfied by the combination of these unit tests and fra-cty's sse-resume.test.ts. Per code review, removed the unused initialSeq parameter from createChatSseSequencer to eliminate a future-misuse vector. 114 chat tests pass.

title: Implement per-thread coordinator serializing turn state

close_reason: Delivered per-thread coordinator serialization with bounded SSE turn retention, lookup-only resume semantics, event-history hardening, and coordinator/http tests. Reviewed after implementation; chat tests passed.

title: Implement shared quote-row skeleton across watchlist + held surfaces

Hydration goes through the existing useFetched hook; presentation uses signedColor.ts. Snapshot test on quoteRowView pins the shared-render contract: same (state, subjectRef) in → same QuoteRowView out, asserted across both surfaces.

Lockstep unnest($2::text[], $3::uuid[]) join keeps the bulk lookup to one round trip. User-scoped — cross-user holdings never leak. Closes parent fra-cw0.9 (.9.1, .9.2, .9.3 all delivered).

Spec §6.2.1 metadata fields (as_of, delay_class, currency, source_id) plus parent-epic move math (prev_close, change_abs, change_pct) are all validated at construction; assertQuoteContract re-asserts the same invariants for any adapter. Polygon adapter migrated from /v2/last/trade to /v2/snapshot to source prev_close in one round trip. New contract.test.ts proves: (a) Polygon adapter satisfies the contract, (b) fixture adapter satisfies the contract, (c) a deliberately-broken adapter is correctly rejected. 20 new tests, all 29 in services/market pass.

title: Bootstrap SSE route stub for chat streaming
description: What: /v1/chat/threads/:threadId/stream route wired as SSE emitting stub events until P2.1. Why: Unblocks frontend streaming work during P0. Contract: Route accepts session; emits heartbeat. Verification: curl -N keeps connection open and emits heartbeats.
close_reason: Completed

title: Document partition strategy for facts and tool_call_logs
description: What: Decision doc + example DDL for monthly partitioning of facts and tool_call_logs. Why: Both tables grow unboundedly. Contract: Doc specifies partition key, retention policy, and cross-partition query pattern. Verification: Doc + sample partition in dev.
close_reason: Added db/docs/partition-strategy.md covering partition keys (as_of for facts, created_at for tool_call_logs), monthly granularity rationale, PK structural change and supersession FK implications, asymmetric retention (facts: detach+archive; tool_call_logs: rolling DROP), and good/bad cross-partition query patterns. Sample DDL in db/docs/examples/ runs cleanly against Postgres 15 — EXPLAIN output verifies pruning on the well-formed query and Append over all partitions on the anti-pattern.

title: Set up CI (typecheck, lint, tests)
description: What: CI running typecheck, lint, tests on every PR. Why: Invariant-sensitive code needs baseline CI. Contract: CI must pass before merge. Verification: A trivial PR runs the full CI suite.
close_reason: Completed: CI workflow runs web typecheck/lint/test plus db and resolver integration tests on PRs; verified on PR #6 checks.

title: Bootstrap observability log tables + write helpers
description: What: tool_call_logs, citation_logs, verifier_fail_logs, eval_run_results writable from backend. Why: Day-one observability catches invariant drift. Contract: Every tool call writes tool_call_logs; every block citation writes citation_logs. Verification: Run any tool call; row appears.
close_reason: Completed: observability tables and write helpers for tool_call_logs, citation_logs, verifier_fail_logs, and eval_run_results are implemented and verified by services/observability tests.

title: Apply finance_research_db_schema.sql to fresh Postgres
description: What: Run the normative schema pack. Why: Foundation for every downstream service. Contract: Schema applies cleanly on Postgres 15+ with pgcrypto. Verification: Migration runs; psql \dt lists all tables from the pack.
close_reason: Added a db package with apply/verify CLIs, plus a Docker-backed Postgres 15 integration test that applies the normative schema and verifies the full table inventory.

title: Implement migration runner + forward/backward migrations
description: What: Migration tool for every schema change after initial apply. Why: Ad-hoc DDL destroys reproducibility and blocks rollback. Contract: Each migration has up() and down(); runner tracks applied set. Verification: Roll forward then backward on a test DB.
close_reason: Added tracked SQL migrations with up/down/status plus Docker-backed forward/backward migration coverage.

title: Seed reference data (metrics registry, common sources)
description: What: Populate metrics with common definitions (revenue, EPS, margins, ratios) + minimal Source registry. Why: Fundamentals + market services reference metric_ids. Contract: Seed is idempotent. Verification: Re-running seed produces no duplicates.
close_reason: Added seed/metrics.sql (45 core metrics) and seed/sources.sql (7-entry source registry with deterministic UUIDs). New seed runner (scripts/seed.ts) applies files transactionally in lexical order. Idempotency covered by db/test/seed.test.ts which verifies count + content digest stable across re-runs and that seeded rows satisfy facts FK constraints.

title: Implement default manual watchlist + membership CRUD
description: What: One implicit default manual watchlist per user. Add/remove/view members only. Why: Later watchlist concepts build on this baseline. Contract: Membership is canonical SubjectRef, idempotent at subject level. Verification: Adding same subject twice yields one row.
close_reason: Shipped the P0.4b default-manual-watchlist + membership-CRUD baseline across DB, backend, and frontend in PR #13. DB migration 0003 provisions the implicit manual list via AFTER INSERT trigger on users plus a unique partial index that enforces at most one manual list per user (fra-wlc tracks multi-list CRUD beyond this baseline). New services/watchlists/ service exposes GET/POST /v1/watchlists/default/members and DELETE /v1/watchlists/default/members/{kind}/{id} with x-user-id header-stub auth; adds are idempotent via on conflict do nothing returning created or already_present. Frontend renders the list in WatchlistSlot with live add/remove via a hoisted useManualWatchlist hook; resolver handoffs from the sidebar search update the list in the same render. OpenAPI documents the new endpoints and WatchlistMember / AddWatchlistMemberResponse schemas. Verified with db migrate tests (7), watchlists HTTP integration tests (9), web unit tests (40), Playwright smoke of the golden path, and all CI jobs green including the new watchlists (integration tests) job.

title: Implement watchlist row quote hydration (reuses P0.4 rule)
description: What: Row hydration pulls the same listing-oriented quote snapshot as P0.4 landing. Why: No second quote identity model for watchlist rows. Contract: Row shape display id + listing context + price + move + pct + freshness. Verification: Row and symbol-detail landing show identical values for same subject.
notes: Added quoteFromSubjectRef helper in web/src/symbol/quote.ts that routes watchlist row hydration through the same createQuoteSnapshotStub used by subject-detail landing. Refactored search.ts to expose subjectFromRef (shared between subjectFromRouteParam and row hydration). Updated ManualWatchlist.tsx to render ticker/MIC + price + signed percent move + freshness title per row with a Link to /symbol/{ref}/overview and a sibling Remove button. Verification: new quote.test.ts equivalence test asserts deepEqual between quoteFromSubjectRef(ref) and createQuoteSnapshotStub(subjectFromRouteParam(subjectRouteParam(ref))) — row and landing share one derivation function.
close_reason: Added quoteFromSubjectRef in web/src/symbol/quote.ts that routes watchlist row hydration through the same createQuoteSnapshotStub that subject detail uses. Factored subjectFromRef out of subjectFromRouteParam in search.ts so the URL-entered landing path and the watchlist row path build the minimal ResolvedSubject the exact same way. ManualWatchlist.tsx now renders each row as a Link(to=symbolDetailPathForSubject) with primary ticker/kind label, secondary MIC·currency, price (formatQuotePrice), signed percent move (direction-colored), and a freshness title attribute; the Remove button is a sibling outside the Link for accessibility. WatchlistSlot.tsx comment updated. Verification: new quote.test.ts equivalence test asserts deepEqual(quoteFromSubjectRef(ref), createQuoteSnapshotStub(subjectFromRouteParam(subjectRouteParam(ref)))) — row and landing share one derivation for the same subject. Live-verified in browser: row listing:1111… showed $108.71 / -1.62%, landing showed $108.71 / -1.79 (-1.62%); issuer:3333… row showed $239.91 / +0.17%, landing showed $239.91 / +0.41 (+0.17%). All 43 web tests pass, typecheck + lint + vite build green.

title: Implement add-to-watchlist with inline auth interrupt
description: What: From a public subject route, 'Add to watchlist' triggers the inline auth interrupt and resumes after sign-in. Why: Videos show this flow. Contract: Unauth click preserves route + pending add; post-sign-in completes. Verification: E2E test with unauth user.
close_reason: Replaced the SaveToWatchlistProtectedAction.payload from {symbol: string} to {subject_ref: SubjectRef, display_name?: string} since the watchlist persists canonical SubjectRefs. SubjectDetailShell now renders a Save-to-watchlist button below the QuoteSnapshot in the public subject-detail header; click fires requestProtectedAction with the resolved subject_ref + display_name. WatchlistSlot registers a useResumedProtectedAction handler for SaveToWatchlist that calls watchlist.addSubject(action.payload.subject_ref) — when an unauth click queues the pending action and the user signs in, AuthInterruptProvider dispatches the resume and the membership add lands automatically. authInterruptState.isProtectedAction validates the subject_ref shape (kind ∈ SUBJECT_KINDS, non-empty id) plus optional string display_name. Retired the HomePage P0.1.3 scaffold (Save AAPL button) — replaced by the real CTA on subject detail per its own deprecation comment. Verification: 45/45 web tests pass (including new round-trip and rejection tests for the new payload shape); typecheck + lint + vite build green; live E2E in browser exercised four flows on /symbol/listing:1111…/overview: (1) unauth click opens modal with 'Saving Listing subject…' message and stores pending action with subject_ref+returnTo; (2) Sign in dispatches and adds to watchlist while preserving the URL; (3) authed click adds immediately without modal; (4) Cancel dismisses modal, clears pending, preserves URL, does not add.

title: Implement shell-owned symbol search entry
description: What: The one symbol search input every surface reuses. Why: Per-surface search reimplementation is how ticker-string shortcuts creep in. Contract: All surfaces target the same component and the same /v1/subjects/resolve call. Verification: Add-to-watchlist and shell header use the same component.
close_reason: Implemented a reusable shell-owned SymbolSearch component wired to /v1/subjects/resolve and reused by the top bar plus watchlist rail.

title: Implement subject-detail shell landing with minimum quote snapshot
description: What: Market identity strip + price-first quote snapshot (price, absolute move, pct, freshness, small chart hook). Why: Immediate landing UX shown in videos. Contract: Quote retrieval is listing-oriented; issuer profile is best-effort non-blocking. Verification: Entering symbol detail shows the landing within one render cycle.
close_reason: Rendered the subject-detail landing with canonical display identity, listing context, price, absolute and percentage move, freshness/session state, and a small recent-range chart.

title: Wire quote hydration via P1.1 stub
description: What: Temporary stub for P1.1 market-data call so landing renders end-to-end during P0. Why: Unblocks P0.4b and P1.3 work without waiting for full market-data service. Contract: Stub returns fixture with correct shape (as_of, delay_class, currency, source_id). Verification: Replacing stub with P1.1 requires no UI change.
close_reason: Added the P1.1-compatible quote snapshot stub with listing-oriented shape including as_of, delay_class, currency, source_id, and recent range data.

title: Implement candidate search -> canonical selection -> hydrated handoff flow
description: What: Three-stage pipeline with auto-advance and explicit-choice paths. Why: Downstream consumers should never reconstruct this. Contract: Unique deterministic hit auto-advances; multiple plausible hits pause at ambiguity; not_found ends. Verification: Flow tests for all three branches.
close_reason: Implemented reusable search-to-subject flow with candidate search, canonical selection, auto-advance hydrated handoff, explicit-choice hydrated handoff, and not_found termination. Added flow tests for unique, ambiguous, explicit choice, and not_found branches. Verified with services/resolver npm test: 70 tests, 44 passed, 26 skipped due Docker unavailable.

title: Implement hydrated subject bundle contract
description: What: Bundle shape carries canonical SubjectRef, identity level, display labels, normalized input, resolution path, and issuer/instrument/listing context. Why: Single shape every subject consumer consumes. Contract: Persisted key is only the SubjectRef; other fields are entry context. Verification: Watchlists, Chat composer, Analyze entry all consume the same shape.
notes: Review finding fix: wired /v1/subjects/resolve through the hydrated search-to-subject flow, added explicit choice support, and extended the OpenAPI response shape for hydrated bundle fields.
close_reason: Fixed review finding by wiring /v1/subjects/resolve to runSearchToSubjectFlow, returning hydrated bundle fields for resolved handoffs, accepting explicit candidate choices for ambiguity, and updating the OpenAPI schema/tests. Verified with services/resolver npm test: 77 tests, 51 passed, 26 skipped due Docker unavailable.

title: Log resolution path (auto_advanced / explicit_choice) to PX.1
description: What: Emit telemetry distinguishing auto-advance from explicit-choice resolutions. Why: Useful signal for resolver quality and UX friction measurement. Contract: Every resolution writes to tool_call_logs with the path. Verification: Query logs by path and see both branches populated.
notes: Review follow-up: added explicit tests that needs_choice and not_found paths do not write resolution telemetry, and documented that telemetry insert failures reject hydrated results to preserve the every-resolution logging contract.
close_reason: Logged every hydrated search-to-subject resolution to tool_call_logs with resolver.search_to_subject_flow, status ok, and JSON args containing resolution_path, normalized_input, subject_ref, and identity_level. Added flow tests for auto_advanced and explicit_choice telemetry. Verified with services/resolver npm test: 79 tests, 53 passed, 26 skipped due Docker unavailable.

title: Implement normalization without identity-level collapse
description: What: Normalizer preserves issuer-vs-instrument-vs-listing distinctions while matching input tokens. Why: Collapsing GOOG/GOOGL or ADR classes hides invariants downstream. Contract: Normalized form never merges rows representing different canonical identities. Verification: 'google' returns separate candidates for issuer vs GOOG vs GOOGL.
close_reason: Added src/normalize.ts with NormalizedQuery returning separate ticker_candidate (case-folded only, preserves every distinguishing character), name_candidate (lowercase, punctuation-stripped, whitespace-collapsed), and identifier_hint (CIK/ISIN/LEI pattern classification; no DB lookup — that's 3.3's job). 15 tests verify the bead's central invariant (GOOG/GOOGL never collapse, BRK.A/BRK.B preserved) plus Unicode safety, CIK padding normalization, ISIN/LEI case-folding, and the 'google' premise that feeds downstream multi-axis matching.

title: Implement ticker/alias/CIK/ISIN entry points promoting to SubjectRef
description: What: Accept multiple input families but always output canonical SubjectRef. Why: Provider payloads arrive with varied id fields. Contract: resolver(ticker='AAPL'), resolver(cik='0000320193'), resolver(isin='US0378331005') all arrive at the same canonical Apple identity. Verification: Tests assert cross-id-family equivalence.
close_reason: Added src/lookup.ts with resolveByTicker / resolveByCik / resolveByIsin / resolveByLei plus a resolveByInput dispatcher over a discriminated-union input. Each hits the canonical DB columns (listings.ticker, issuers.cik/lei, instruments.isin) and returns a ResolverEnvelope. Unique-identifier hits get confidence 0.99; ticker single-match 0.95; ticker ambiguity 0.5 each with multiple_listings or multiple_issuers axis based on whether distinct issuer_ids were involved. 6 Docker-backed integration tests cover the bead's core cross-family equivalence (ticker/CIK/ISIN/LEI all trace to the same Apple issuer), CIK leading-zero normalization, case-insensitive ISIN/LEI, unknown → not_found with structured reason, MIC filtering, and the multiple_issuers axis when historical ticker reuse is present.

title: Wire /v1/subjects/resolve handler per OpenAPI
description: What: HTTP endpoint matching finance_research_openapi.yaml. Why: Frontend only talks to /v1/*; no direct client-to-provider calls. Contract: POST /v1/subjects/resolve accepts {text, allow_kinds?} returns envelope. Verification: Contract test against OpenAPI schema.
close_reason: Added src/http.ts with the POST /v1/subjects/resolve handler per spec OpenAPI. Four components: validateResolveRequest (shape-check: text required, allow_kinds must be SubjectKind[]), dispatchFreeText (normalize → identifier_hint route → ticker_candidate fallback; name lookup deliberately deferred to fra-6al.4), envelopeToSubjects (maps internal 3-way envelope to flat {subjects[], unresolved[]} shape preserving no-silent-winner via per-candidate confidence), and createResolverServer (node:http createServer binding handler to POST /v1/subjects/resolve; 404 for everything else). 14 tests: validation unit tests, handler-level coverage for ticker/CIK/unknown/ambiguous/allow_kinds, and end-to-end server tests that fetch the endpoint and assert the OpenAPI-shaped {subjects[], unresolved[]} response body.

title: Implement primary workspace routes (/home, /agents, /chat, /screener, /analyze)
description: What: Route groups inside the shell. Chat is thread-scoped (/chat/:threadId). Why: These are app navigation routes, not /v1/* endpoint groups. Contract: All five primary routes render inside the same shell. Verification: URL changes on nav; shell chrome does not remount.
close_reason: Implemented thread-scoped Chat routing. /chat now has an index child (ChatEmptyState) and a :threadId child (ChatThreadView) rendered inside a ChatLayout parent that hosts an <Outlet />. Final auth model uses route metadata, not ProtectedSurface: the /chat parent route declares handle: { scope: 'protected', label: 'Chat' }, and RouteScopeGate applies the gate to both /chat and /chat/:threadId through inherited scope. Other four primary routes (/home, /agents, /screener, /analyze) already shipped in fra-6al.1.1. Verified in browser: unauthed /chat and /chat/abc123 both gate; authed shows empty state on /chat and thread view on /chat/xyz789; in-app navigation between them preserves the session and the shell chrome (no remount).

comments.author: David Ten
comments.text: Refresh close reason to match the final route-scope metadata model.

title: Implement entered subject-detail shell with nested sections
description: What: /symbol/:subjectRef/{overview|financials|earnings|holders|signals} — main canvas swaps into a subject-detail shell while outer shell persists. Why: Subject sections are durable nested routes, not dynamic tabs. Contract: The subject-detail shell owns shared subject header context and local section navigation. Verification: Overview -> financials preserves subject context and shell chrome.
close_reason: Implemented entered subject-detail shell. /symbol/:subjectRef renders SubjectDetailShell with subject header + local section nav (Overview/Financials/Earnings/Holders/Signals) + Outlet; index redirects to /overview. All five sections scaffolded in pages/symbol/sections.tsx, each pointing at the bead that fills real content. Public route per spec §3.11 (no ProtectedSurface wrap). URL uses canonical :subjectRef (not :ticker) per spec §3.8. Verified in browser: (1) /symbol/us-aapl-common index redirects to /overview; (2) section switches (Overview -> Financials) preserve the shell header (same ref on h1 across snapshots); (3) outer shell chrome (watchlist, top bar, primary tabs, activity rail) refs identical across section switches AND subject↔primary navigations, proving zero remount; (4) no primary tab shows as active while on /symbol/*, matching the 'entered route group, not a primary workspace' semantics.

title: Implement session-scope route metadata (public vs protected)
description: What: Route table carries public/protected flags consumed by the in-shell auth gate. Why: Route-level metadata is how the gate knows when to show. Contract: Public: Home, Screener, Analyze-entry, entered symbol-detail. Protected: Chat, Agents, user-owned watchlists, persisted Analyze runs. Verification: Unauthed /home renders; unauthed /chat renders auth gate.
close_reason: Route-level scope metadata. Each route declares handle: { scope: 'public' | 'protected', label? }; RouteScopeGate (lives in WorkspaceShell's main) reads useMatches() and renders AuthGate in place of Outlet when the deepest matched handle is protected and session is null. Per-route <ProtectedSurface> wrapper retired (file deleted). Migrated to createBrowserRouter + RouterProvider because useMatches requires the v7 data-router API. Spec §3.10 scope assignments baked in: Home/Screener/Analyze/Symbol = public; Agents/Chat (+ /chat/:threadId inheriting) = protected. Verified full matrix in browser: public routes render unauthed, protected gate with correct label, child routes inherit parent scope (no child-level handle needed), sign-in from gate resumes to /chat/abc123 thread view. Build + lint green.

title: Implement resolver envelope (resolved / ambiguous / not_found)
description: What: Typed response envelope the resolver returns with three outcomes — resolved, ambiguous, not_found. Why: Ambiguity-preserving contracts are the only way to avoid silent winner-picking. Contract: Each resolution returns exactly one of three outcomes with ranked candidates when ambiguous. Verification: 'GOOG' returns ranked candidates; 'NOTREAL' returns not_found; 'AAPL' returns resolved with canonical listing.
close_reason: Added services/resolver with the three-outcome envelope (resolved/ambiguous/not_found) as a discriminated union plus factory functions that enforce the invariants TS can't (confidence in [0,1], ambiguous requires >=2 candidates, candidates ranked desc, alternatives cannot out-rank the chosen target). 12 Node-native tests cover each outcome shape, every invariant rejection, type-guard exclusivity, and constructibility of the bead verification examples (GOOG ambiguous, AAPL resolved with canonical listing, NOTREAL not_found). Lookup, normalization, and HTTP wiring are deliberately out of scope — those are fra-6al.3.2/.3/.4.

title: Implement persistent workspace shell with three regions
description: What: Left nav, main canvas, right-rail slot in one component that persists across primary-workspace transitions. Why: The shell is the durable navigation frame regardless of auth; surface swaps happen inside it. Contract: Shell chrome never unmounts on workspace transitions. Verification: Navigating Home -> Chat -> Screener keeps nav selection state and does not flash the shell.
close_reason: Shell with three persistent regions implemented; invariant verified via Playwright (nav tag survived /home -> /chat -> /screener); build + typecheck pass. See commit 4cea911.

title: Implement in-shell auth gate (soft guards, not redirects)
description: What: Protected workspaces render an auth gate in the main canvas instead of redirecting. Why: Redirects break the persistent-shell contract and lose return-to context. Contract: Session loss inside a protected surface collapses content to the auth gate; public surfaces keep rendering. Verification: Log out while on /chat/:id — Chat pane gates, Home is unaffected.
close_reason: Implemented in-shell auth gate (soft guards, no redirects). Added AuthProvider (mock identity-agnostic session), ProtectedSurface guard, AuthGate main-canvas UI, SessionControl affordance. Chat and Agents wrapped with ProtectedSurface. Verified in browser: unauthenticated /chat entry preserves URL and shell chrome while collapsing main canvas to the gate; sign-out while on /chat collapses Chat in place; Home keeps rendering throughout. Lint and build green.

title: Implement inline auth interrupt (preserves route + pending action)
description: What: Public routes launching protected actions preserve the current route and pending-action payload so sign-in resumes the action. Why: Videos show save-to-watchlist from public pages; forcing route change breaks flow. Contract: After sign-in the user returns to the same public route and the pending action completes. Verification: Unauth click 'Add to watchlist' -> sign in -> add completes without leaving the page.
close_reason: Implemented inline auth interrupt. AuthInterruptProvider owns pending action slot; session null→truthy transition flushes pending via useEffect (so modal Sign-in + any future programmatic session restore both resume the action). AuthInterrupt modal mounted in shell. Demo consumer on HomePage (Save AAPL to watchlist) verifies all four paths: unauthed→modal→signin→action fires, authed→immediate fire, cancel→discard, URL never changes. Lint and build green.

title: Adopt serializeJsonValue/serializeNullableJsonValue in themes + analyze repos
description:
From fra-ast review: services/themes/src/theme-repo.ts:87 and services/analyze/src/template-repo.ts use raw JSON.stringify for jsonb columns instead of the validation-aware helpers in services/observability/src/types.ts (serializeJsonValue, serializeNullableJsonValue).

Why this matters:
The helpers run assertJsonValue at the boundary, which catches:
- bigint values (would become 'NaN' or throw at pg with a worse message)
- non-finite numbers (Infinity, NaN — pg jsonb rejects NaN with a poor error)
- circular references
- non-plain objects (class instances, Map/Set)
- functions/symbols/undefined

services/observability/src/{tool-call,eval-run,agent-run,verifier-fail}.ts and services/chat/src/messages.ts already use these. Themes and analyze drifted.

Files to touch:
- services/themes/src/theme-repo.ts:87 (membership_spec)
- services/analyze/src/template-repo.ts:102-106 (source_categories, added_subject_refs, optional jsonb columns in createAnalyzeTemplate)
- services/analyze/src/template-repo.ts:171-175 (same columns in updateAnalyzeTemplate via serializePatchJson)
- services/analyze/src/template-repo.ts:255 (serializeOptionalJson — fold into the helper)
- Add unit tests asserting the helpers reject the listed bad shapes at each call site.

Acceptance: All jsonb writes from themes and analyze repos route through the centralized serialize helpers; no remaining direct JSON.stringify calls for jsonb binds in those two repos.

title: Disambiguate 'transform' refresh reason from unknown-response fallback
description:
Currently parseBlockSnapshotTransformResponse falls back to {state:'refresh_required', reason:'transform'} for both:
1. Legitimate 409 with reason=transform (snapshot rejected the transform).
2. Malformed/unknown server responses (5xx, missing envelope, made-up reason).

Monitoring can't distinguish wire-format breaks from legitimate transform-rejected outcomes. Add a separate parsed-response variant (e.g., reason: 'unknown') so observability can split them; refresh prompt copy can stay shared if desired.

Files to touch:
- web/src/chat/blockSnapshotTransform.ts
- web/src/chat/blockSnapshotTransform.test.ts
- web/src/chat/snapshotRefreshPromptCopy.ts (optional copy decision)
- web/src/chat/snapshotRefreshPromptCopy.test.ts

From code review of fra-7vn.3 (commit 551d012).
close_reason: Superseded by PR #41 review fix (CodeRabbit flagged the same disambiguation as a Major issue). parseBlockSnapshotTransformResponse now returns a distinct unexpected_error state for 401/403/5xx and malformed 409 envelopes, instead of the overloaded transform fallback.

title: Make citation block logging atomic
description: writeCitationLogsForBlocks currently inserts extracted citation rows one by one, so a later insert failure can leave earlier rows persisted. Add transaction/prevalidation semantics or document a required caller transaction boundary so block citation logging cannot partially persist.
acceptance_criteria: A failing writeCitationLogsForBlocks call cannot leave a partially written citation_logs set for a block batch, or the helper requires and verifies an explicit caller transaction boundary; tests cover the failure path.
close_reason: Resolved in PR #36 review follow-up: writeCitationLogsForBlocks now performs one atomic insert statement for block-derived citation rows, with tests covering single-statement batch writes.

title: Implement watchlist list-management CRUD beyond P0.4b baseline
description: What: Create, rename, and delete named manual watchlists; support multiple named lists per user; optional sharing and reordering. Why: P0.4b (fra-6al.6) baseline is deliberately membership-only on the implicit default list; this bead captures the deferred list-object CRUD so OpenAPI's existing POST /v1/watchlists (and related endpoints) are consciously scoped rather than dangling. Contract: Each list is a user-owned named collection with a mode. Multiple lists per user; default remains the implicit 'manual' list. Rename and delete preserve member rows or cascade per product decision made in this bead. Verification: Can create a named manual list, rename it, list all of a user's lists, and delete a non-default list; attempting to delete the default returns a typed error. Scope note: this bead intentionally supersedes the parent fra-6al.6 exclusion list item ('This bead does not define create-list, rename-list, delete-list, sharing, reordering, or multiple manual lists.').
